title: Russian missile strike on Dnipro kills civilians
detail: A Russian missile attack on Dnipro hit civilian areas and infrastructure, part of the sustained nationwide pressure campaign on Ukrainian cities.
dl: MODERATE — Civilian districts damaged

title: Ukraine drone attack targets Tatarstan industrial facilities
detail: Long-range Ukrainian drones struck deep inside Russia, underscoring Kyiv's focus on oil, industrial, and military-linked targets beyond the front.
dl: LIGHT — Deep-strike disruption inside Russia

title: Russian glide bombs and missiles hit Kharkiv region
detail: Russian aviation and missiles intensified attacks on Kharkiv region, continuing the pattern of cross-border bombardment and pressure on northeastern Ukraine.
dl: MODERATE — Urban and energy damage mounting

title: Russia opens new push in Kharkiv border zone
detail: Russian forces pressed attacks in the Kharkiv border area, threatening settlements north of the city and forcing evacuations.
dl: HEAVY — Frontier settlements contested

title: Major Russian strike wave hits Kyiv and central Ukraine
detail: Russia launched one of its larger overnight drone and missile barrages against Kyiv and multiple regions, damaging homes and critical infrastructure.
dl: HEAVY — Capital struck in mass barrage

title: Ukraine hits Russian oil depot in border region
detail: Ukrainian drones targeted a Russian fuel and logistics site in support of Kyiv's campaign against oil-linked war infrastructure.
dl: MODERATE — Fuel storage disrupted

title: Russian strike damages Odesa port facilities
detail: Russian drones and missiles hit Odesa region, including port-linked infrastructure central to Ukraine's maritime exports.
dl: HEAVY — Export infrastructure damaged

title: Russia pounds Kyiv in large missile and drone attack
detail: A broad Russian aerial assault hit Kyiv and other cities, continuing Moscow's strategy of exhausting Ukrainian air defence and striking urban systems.
dl: HEAVY — Residential and utility damage

title: Ukraine drones hit Russian Black Sea and energy targets
detail: Kyiv expanded attacks on Russian military and fuel targets connected to the southern war effort and Black Sea operations.
dl: MODERATE — Naval and energy disruption

title: Ukraine launches cross-border incursion into Kursk region
detail: Ukrainian forces crossed into Russia's Kursk region in a surprise operation, seizing villages and forcing Moscow to divert troops.
dl: CRITICAL — Frontline penetrated inside Russia

title: Russia counterattacks against Ukrainian positions in Kursk
detail: Russian troops and aviation counterattacked in Kursk as Moscow sought to contain Ukraine's incursion and restore control over border settlements.
dl: HEAVY — Counteroffensive underway

title: Russia launches one of biggest attacks on Ukrainian energy grid
detail: A mass Russian missile and drone strike targeted power infrastructure across Ukraine, causing outages and renewed strain on the grid.
dl: CRITICAL — Nationwide power infrastructure hit

title: Ukraine strikes Moscow-area and refinery targets with drones
detail: Ukrainian drones targeted Moscow-region air defences and Russian oil infrastructure as Kyiv sustained pressure on rear-area logistics.
dl: MODERATE — Airspace and fuel systems pressured

title: Russia launches 595 drones and 48 missiles at Ukraine
detail: One of the largest reported Russian aerial attacks of the war targeted Kyiv and multiple regions with hundreds of drones and dozens of missiles.
dl: CRITICAL — Massive nationwide strike wave

title: Russian forces advance in Donetsk sector
detail: Russian troops continued grinding offensives in Donetsk, threatening key Ukrainian logistics towns and compounding pressure on the eastern front.
dl: HEAVY — Frontline pressure intensifies

title: Ukraine attacks Russian refinery and arms-linked sites
detail: Kyiv carried out another long-range drone wave against refinery and military-industrial targets inside Russia.
dl: MODERATE — Refining capacity disrupted

title: Russia renews sweeping attacks on Ukrainian power and cities
detail: A fresh nationwide strike package hit electricity and civilian systems as winter approached, reviving blackout concerns.
dl: CRITICAL — Winter energy campaign escalates

title: Ukraine drones hit Russian oil and military depots
detail: Ukrainian strikes again targeted Russia's oil-fed war economy and ammunition support network.
dl: MODERATE — Rear-area depots disrupted

title: Escalating Russian strikes target Odesa ports and bridge links
detail: Russia intensified attacks on Odesa-region maritime infrastructure in an effort to squeeze Ukraine's sea access and trade flows.
dl: HEAVY — Port throughput threatened

title: Russian air attack causes outages across Ukraine
detail: Russian forces hit energy and military facilities in another coordinated strike, sparking sweeping outages and continuing winter pressure on civilians.
dl: HEAVY — Blackout pressure renewed

title: Ukraine strikes Russian oil and gas facilities
detail: Ukraine hit Russian energy facilities with drones and Western-supplied weapons in a bid to reduce revenues funding the war.
dl: HEAVY — Energy revenue targets hit

title: Russian drones and missiles pound Ukraine before diplomacy push
detail: Russia launched another heavy aerial attack as diplomatic efforts stalled, illustrating Moscow's reliance on pressure through long-range strikes.
dl: HEAVY — Air defence saturation continues

title: Ukraine sustains pressure on Russian rear-area infrastructure
detail: Kyiv continued its deep-strike campaign against fuel, rail, and storage targets that support Russian operations at the front.
dl: MODERATE — Logistics friction increased

title: Russia batters Zaporizhzhia and frontline cities
detail: Russian missiles, drones and glide bombs hit southeastern Ukrainian cities and frontline communities under persistent pressure.
dl: MODERATE — Civilian and infrastructure damage

title: Russian forces keep grinding forward in Donbas
detail: Russia maintained incremental advances around eastern strongpoints while coupling ground assaults with air and artillery pressure.
dl: HEAVY — Attritional offensives continue

title: Ukraine hits Moscow region in large drone raid
detail: A large Ukrainian drone operation targeted the Moscow region and other sites, signaling Kyiv's ability to hold politically sensitive Russian areas at risk.
dl: MODERATE — Capital region disrupted

title: Russian mass drone attack hits central and western Ukraine
detail: Russia launched another broad drone wave against multiple Ukrainian regions, keeping steady pressure on infrastructure and civilians.
dl: MODERATE — Distributed nationwide damage

title: Russian attacks continue as front and air war grind on
detail: Russia maintained pressure with drone, missile and frontline attacks across Ukraine while Kyiv continued to absorb and answer with long-range strikes.
dl: MODERATE — War remains in sustained high-intensity phase

title: Easter ceasefire announced as Kyiv presses for longer pause
detail: Putin announced a 32-hour Orthodox Easter ceasefire and Zelenskiy said Ukraine would observe it, while Kyiv urged Moscow to extend the pause and restart talks rather than resume attacks after the holiday.
dl: LIGHT — Short humanitarian truce announced amid stalled peace efforts

title: Ukraine and Russia swap 175 POWs each before Easter truce
detail: Kyiv and Moscow exchanged 175 prisoners of war each, one of the few concrete outcomes linked to U.S.-brokered contacts, with Ukraine also receiving seven civilians and Russia taking back seven civilians from Kursk region.
dl: LIGHT — Major prisoner exchange completed

title: Easter truce quickly frays amid border drone strikes and front-line fire
detail: Hours after the ceasefire began, both sides accused each other of violations. Russian officials reported Ukrainian drone strikes in Kursk and Belgorod, while Ukraine's military said Russian forces committed hundreds of truce breaches including assaults, shelling and drone attacks.
dl: MODERATE — Ceasefire undermined almost immediately

title: Orthodox Easter ceasefire collapses under widespread shelling and drone attacks
detail: Russia and Ukraine accused each other of breaching the 32-hour Easter truce, but Ukrainian military reporting described thousands of Russian violations by the time the pause expired, including artillery fire, assault actions and attack-drone strikes across the front while civilians were reported hurt in Kharkiv region.
dl: MODERATE — Holiday truce broke down almost completely before expiry

title: Russian troops regain ground in the Oleksandrivka sector near the oblast tri-border
detail: ISW assessed that Russian forces recently advanced in the Oleksandrivka direction along the Zaporizhzhia-Dnipropetrovsk-Donetsk border area, showing Moscow can still recover some ground even as Ukrainian counterattacks have disrupted this sector in recent weeks.
dl: MODERATE — Russian localized advance on the southeastern front

title: Ukrainian forces make a localized advance in the Slovyansk direction
detail: The Institute for the Study of War assessed that Ukrainian forces recently advanced on the Slovyansk axis, signaling continued counterpressure on a sector where Russia has been trying to build momentum toward the Slovyansk-Kramatorsk line.
dl: LIGHT — Local Ukrainian gain on the eastern front

title: Ukrainian drone strike sparks fires at Cherepovets Azot chemical plant
detail: Geolocated footage and regional reporting indicated a Ukrainian long-range drone strike set fires at the Cherepovets Azot plant in Vologda Oblast, part of Kyiv's continuing campaign against Russian industrial infrastructure supporting the war effort.
dl: MODERATE — Deep-rear industrial target hit in northwestern Russia

title: Russian missile strike kills civilians in Dnipro
detail: A Russian missile strike on Dnipro killed at least five civilians, wounded more than two dozen people, and damaged transport-linked infrastructure and nearby civilian property in the southeastern city.
dl: MODERATE — Civilian traffic and infrastructure hit in a daytime strike

title: Ukraine and Germany sign major air-defence and drone production package
detail: Kyiv and Berlin signed a new defence package worth about €4 billion, including several hundred Patriot missiles, additional IRIS-T launchers, financing for deep-strike capabilities, and joint production of thousands of mid-strike drones.
dl: LIGHT — Major military aid and joint-production package agreed

title: Ukraine unveils drone-assault combat model and says it retook territory in March
detail: Ukraine's Defence Ministry said it is introducing integrated drone-assault units that combine aerial and ground unmanned systems with infantry, and Commander-in-Chief Oleksandr Syrskyi said Ukrainian forces regained nearly 50 square kilometres of territory in March while disrupting Russia's spring offensive pressure.
dl: LIGHT — Ukraine reports localized territorial recovery and a new combat model

title: Ukrainian drone strike reaches Sterlitamak petrochemical plant in Bashkortostan
detail: ISW reported that Ukrainian forces struck the Sterlitamak Petrochemical Plant in Bashkortostan, roughly 1,500 kilometres from the border, as part of Kyiv's long-range campaign against Russian defence-industrial and fuel-related infrastructure.
dl: MODERATE — Deep strike on Russian petrochemical infrastructure

title: Britain announces its largest-ever annual drone package for Ukraine
detail: ISW reported that the United Kingdom will provide at least 120,000 drones to Ukraine in 2026, including long-range strike, reconnaissance, logistics, and maritime systems, with deliveries already beginning in April.
dl: LIGHT — Major allied drone-supply expansion announced

title: Russia launches 324 drones and missiles in mass overnight barrage
detail: Russia launched 324 drones and three ballistic missiles against Ukraine overnight, with impacts reported in Odesa region port infrastructure, Dnipro, Cherkasy, Zaporizhzhia, and the Kyiv region despite extensive Ukrainian interceptions.
dl: HEAVY — Port facilities and multiple cities hit in a countrywide barrage

title: Ukrainian drones strike Tuapse port and oil infrastructure on Russia's Black Sea coast
detail: Ukraine struck infrastructure in and around Russia's Black Sea port of Tuapse, where officials reported fatalities, a fire, damage in the port area, and impacts involving oil-linked facilities. Reuters and ISW both identified Tuapse as part of Ukraine's continuing long-range campaign against Russian fuel and logistics infrastructure supporting the war effort.
dl: HEAVY — Major Black Sea port and oil hub struck in Russia

title: Russia's deadliest aerial attack of 2026 hits Kyiv, Odesa and Dnipro
detail: Russia launched more than 700 drones and missiles during a prolonged strike series, killing at least 17 people and wounding about 100 across Ukraine, with some of the heaviest damage in Kyiv, Odesa, and Dnipro. Ukrainian and ISW reporting highlighted the scale of the barrage and Russia's tactic of using layered waves to exhaust air defences before ballistic missile strikes.
dl: CRITICAL — Worst nationwide strike wave of the year hits major cities

title: Ukrainian forces make localized gains near Dobropillya and Slovyansk
detail: ISW assessed that Ukrainian forces recently advanced near Slovyansk and in the Dobropillya tactical area, indicating continued localized counterpressure on key eastern approaches as Russia sustains its broader Donetsk offensive effort.
dl: LIGHT — Local Ukrainian advance on the eastern front

title: Moscow escalates warnings over European drone production for Ukraine
detail: The Kremlin and former president Dmitry Medvedev publicly warned that expanding European drone manufacturing and supply chains for Ukraine could make related facilities potential Russian military targets, marking a sharper rhetorical escalation against Europe-based defence support for Kyiv.
dl: LIGHT — Russia sharpens threats against Europe-based support for Ukraine

title: Russian drones hit Izmail's Danube port and breach Romanian airspace
detail: A Russian overnight attack damaged administrative, production, and railway infrastructure at Izmail, Ukraine's largest Danube River port, while Romanian radar tracked one drone crossing briefly into Romanian airspace near the border. The strike extended several consecutive days of pressure on Ukraine's southwest logistics corridor.
dl: MODERATE — Danube logistics hub hit again and NATO-border airspace violated

title: Russian drone wave damages Odesa port infrastructure and knocks out power in Chernihiv region
detail: Russia launched 219 long-range drones overnight, damaging port infrastructure, warehouses, depots, and administrative buildings in Ukraine's Odesa region while also hitting an energy facility in Chernihiv region and causing blackouts for about 380,000 consumers. The strike extended Moscow's pressure campaign against Ukrainian logistics and power infrastructure after the larger nationwide barrages earlier in the week.
dl: MODERATE — Port and power infrastructure hit in renewed overnight drone attack

title: Ukrainian drones hit Samara refineries, Crimea fuel depot, and Baltic export port
detail: Ukrainian long-range drones struck multiple oil and fuel-linked targets across Russia and occupied Crimea, including refineries at Novokuibyshevsk and Syzran in Samara region, a fuel tank fire in Sevastopol, and the Lukoil terminal at Vysotsk port near St Petersburg. Reuters reported the attacks as part of Kyiv's intensified campaign against Russian oil logistics and export infrastructure that supports Moscow's war budget.
dl: HEAVY — Coordinated deep strike hits Russian oil export and refining network

title: Ukrainian drones hit Tuapse port again, reigniting fires at a key Black Sea oil hub
detail: A renewed Ukrainian drone attack struck Russia's Black Sea port of Tuapse only hours after an earlier blaze had been extinguished, killing at least one person, damaging transport infrastructure, and setting the port area on fire again. Reuters described Tuapse as a major oil product export hub and home to a Rosneft refinery, underscoring Kyiv's continuing campaign against Russian fuel and logistics infrastructure.
dl: MODERATE — Follow-on strike re-hits a major Russian Black Sea export and refining node

title: Russia says its forces are pressing into Kostiantynivka and closing on the Donbas fortress belt
detail: Russia's top general Valery Gerasimov said Russian forces had seized 1,700 square km of Ukrainian territory since the start of 2026, were already fighting in parts of Kostiantynivka, and had moved to within roughly 7 to 12 km of Sloviansk and Kramatorsk. Reuters could not verify the battlefield claims, but the statement marked a significant Russian assertion of momentum against Ukraine's key Donbas defensive line.
dl: MODERATE — Russia claims new pressure on Ukraine's main Donbas defensive belt

title: EU signals imminent approval of a 90 billion euro loan package for Ukraine
detail: EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas said she expected positive decisions within a day on a 90 billion euro loan for Ukraine, while ministers also pushed forward work on a 20th sanctions package against Russia. The package would be a major financial and political reinforcement for Kyiv at a moment of acute wartime funding pressure.
dl: LIGHT — Major EU financing package moves toward approval for Ukraine

title: Ukrainian drones hit a Druzhba-linked oil pumping station in Samara region
detail: Ukrainian drones struck an oil-pumping and dispatch facility in Prosvet, Samara region, which a Ukrainian security source said was part of the Druzhba pipeline supply chain. Reuters reported the strike caused a fire and reportedly damaged five large crude storage tanks, highlighting Kyiv's continued effort to disrupt Russian oil logistics tied to the war economy.
dl: MODERATE — Ukrainian deep strike targets Russian pipeline-linked oil logistics

title: ISW says Ukrainian forces likely liberated Andriivka in northern Sumy Oblast
detail: The Institute for the Study of War reported that geolocated footage published on April 21 showed Ukrainian forces holding positions in northern Andriivka, indicating that Ukraine likely retook the village north of Sumy city. The assessed advance marked a localized Ukrainian territorial gain along the northern border while Russian units continued infiltration attempts in the area.
dl: LIGHT — Localized Ukrainian advance near the Sumy border line

title: Ukraine warns Russian missiles and drones are repeatedly flying near Chornobyl and Khmelnytskyi nuclear sites
detail: Reuters reported Ukrainian Prosecutor General Ruslan Kravchenko saying Russia has repeatedly routed drones and Kinzhal missiles close to the Chornobyl site and the Khmelnytskyi nuclear plant, with dozens of flights recorded and several missile impacts near Khmelnytskyi. Kyiv framed the pattern as intimidation that raises the risk of a nuclear safety incident far beyond the immediate battlefield.
dl: LIGHT — Ukraine flags repeated Russian missile and drone routes near nuclear infrastructure

title: EU ambassadors approve 90 billion euro Ukraine loan and a new sanctions package on Russia
detail: Reuters reported that EU ambassadors approved the disbursement of a promised 90 billion euro loan to Ukraine and agreed a new package of sanctions against Russia after Hungary lifted its veto. The move represented one of the bloc's most consequential recent financial and political decisions in support of Kyiv.
dl: LIGHT — EU clears major new financing and sanctions package tied to the war

title: Russian forces press deeper along the Sumy border near Myropilske
detail: ISW reported Ukrainian officials saying Russian attacks were most intense along the southern Sumy Oblast border, where Russian forces had seized Myropilske and advanced two to three kilometers deep in some areas. The development marked continued pressure on Ukraine's northern border as Moscow works to create a buffer zone east and southeast of Sumy city.
dl: MODERATE — Russian border advance increases pressure east of Sumy

title: Russian drone strike on Dnipro apartment block kills three
detail: A Russian overnight drone attack hit a residential area in Dnipro, partially wrecking a 13-storey apartment building and setting fires at nearby administrative, commercial, and vehicle sites. Ukrainian officials said three people were killed and 10 injured, including two children, underscoring the continuing civilian toll of Russia's long-range strike campaign.
dl: MODERATE — Apartment block badly damaged; three civilians killed

title: Ukrainian drones strike Russian oil infrastructure in Nizhny Novgorod and Samara oblasts
detail: ISW reported that Ukrainian SBU and Special Operations Forces drones struck the Gorky oil pumping station near Neshikha in Nizhny Novgorod Oblast, damaging three oil tanks, while Russian regional reporting acknowledged damage to industrial facilities including in Novokuibyshevsk, Samara Oblast. The attacks continued Kyiv's campaign against Russian oil and petrochemical logistics supporting the war economy.
tn: Gorky oil pumping station / Novokuibyshevsk industrial sites
dl: MODERATE — Ukrainian deep strikes damage Russian oil infrastructure

title: Ukraine and Russia exchange 193 POWs each in continuing Easter swap
detail: Ukraine and Russia exchanged 193 prisoners of war each on April 24, the 73rd exchange since the start of the full-scale invasion and a continuation of the earlier Easter exchange. Kyiv said the returned Ukrainians included service members from multiple branches, wounded soldiers, officers, and POWs who had faced Russian criminal cases or unlawful detention in Chechnya; Ukrainian officials said the United States and United Arab Emirates helped facilitate the swap.
dl: LIGHT — Major humanitarian prisoner exchange returns 193 Ukrainian POWs

title: Ukraine confirms Neptune missiles destroyed workshops at Taganrog drone factory
detail: Ukraine's General Staff confirmed battle-damage results from the April 19 Neptune missile strike on the Atlant Aero plant in Taganrog, Rostov Oblast, saying two workshops were destroyed and four other buildings damaged. The facility reportedly produced Molniya-class strike and reconnaissance drones and components for Orion UAVs, making the confirmed damage a notable hit on Russia's drone production base.
dl: MODERATE — Confirmed damage to Russian drone-production workshops

title: Russian drone attack on Odesa kills married couple and hits residential buildings and a merchant ship
detail: Russian drones struck Odesa overnight, damaging at least three residential buildings and hitting a merchant ship bound for the Greater Odesa ports. Ukrainian authorities said a married couple, both aged 75, was killed and 15 people were injured; emergency crews evacuated dozens of residents, including children, and the ship's crew extinguished an onboard fire without reported casualties.
tn: Odesa residential districts and port approaches
dl: MODERATE — Residential buildings damaged; two civilians killed and merchant ship hit

title: Ukrainian drones reportedly reach Russia's Urals for the first time
detail: Ukrinform cited Ukrainian defense adviser Serhii Sternenko saying Ukrainian drones reached the Urals for the first time, flying more than 1,800 kilometers toward Yekaterinburg and Chelyabinsk. Sternenko said Russian electronic warfare redirected one drone into a residential building in Yekaterinburg, injuring six people, while Russian commentators complained that the drones flew for roughly 10 hours without an air-defense response — a notable expansion in the apparent reach of Ukraine's long-range drone campaign.
dl: MODERATE — Ukrainian drones reportedly set new long-range reach into the Urals

title: Combined Russian strike kills two civilians in Chernihiv Oblast
detail: Russian missiles and Geranium drones struck communities across Chernihiv Oblast overnight, killing two men in Nizhyn and injuring at least seven people across the region. Regional officials reported damage to homes in Horodnia and Snovsk communities and to a hospital and boiler room in Semenivka, with 27 shelling incidents and 54 explosions recorded over the previous 24 hours.
dl: MODERATE — Two civilians killed; homes and hospital infrastructure damaged

title: Ukraine says drones damaged four Russian fighter jets at Shagol airfield
detail: Ukraine's Unmanned Systems Forces commander Robert "Magyar" Brovdi confirmed on May 1 that an April 25 long-range drone strike damaged four Russian fighter jets at Shagol airfield in Chelyabinsk, about 1,700 kilometers from Ukrainian-held territory. The reported damaged aircraft included two Su-57 stealth fighters, one Su-34 fighter-bomber, and another unspecified aircraft; Ukraine's General Staff had earlier said a Su-57 and Su-34 were destroyed, while the updated report said the damage was more extensive.
dl: HEAVY — Deep strike reportedly damages Su-57 and Su-34 aircraft 1,700 km from Ukraine

title: Russia launches massive 666-target missile and drone strike centered on Dnipro
detail: Russia launched one of its largest recent combined aerial attacks from the evening of April 24 into April 25, using 47 missiles and 619 drones against Dnipro and other regions. Ukrainian officials said air defenses neutralized 610 targets, but strikes hit 23 locations; the Kyiv Independent reported at least seven people killed and 45 injured nationwide, with Dnipro's residential and industrial areas bearing the main impact, alongside damage in Kharkiv, Chernihiv, Sumy, Odesa, Kyiv, Mykolaiv, and Cherkasy regions.
dl: HEAVY — Nationwide combined strike kills at least seven and injures dozens

title: SBU drones damage Russian ships, radar sites, and MiG-31 in occupied Crimea
detail: Ukraine's SBU said Alpha special forces drones struck the Russian Black Sea Fleet base in Sevastopol and Belbek airfield overnight on April 26. Reported targets included the large landing ships Yamal and Filchenkov, reconnaissance ship Ivan Khurs, the Lukomka training center, an air-defense radio-technical reconnaissance headquarters, an MR-10M1 Mys-M1 radar station, a MiG-31 aircraft, and Belbek's technical-operational unit. Russian-installed authorities claimed 71 drones were downed and reported one civilian killed and four injured amid debris damage in Sevastopol.
tn: Sevastopol and Belbek airfield, occupied Crimea
dl: HEAVY — Ships, airfield assets, radar, and reconnaissance infrastructure reportedly hit

title: Ukrainian drones hit Yaroslavl oil refinery and Cherepovets chemical plant
detail: Ukraine's General Staff said drones struck the strategically important Yaroslavl oil refinery overnight on April 26, igniting a fire at a facility with around 15 million tons of annual refining capacity. Russian officials and local reports also described a deep strike on the Cherepovets branch of Apatit in Vologda Oblast, one of Europe's largest chemical plants, where a burst high-pressure pipeline caused acid burns; Russian reports put injuries at up to 10 people. Russia's Defense Ministry claimed 203 drones were intercepted across multiple regions and occupied Crimea.
tn: Yaroslavl oil refinery / Cherepovets Apatit plant
dl: HEAVY — Deep strikes hit major refinery and chemical-production sites inside Russia

title: Ukraine hits Tuapse refinery again and strikes Iskander storage in occupied Crimea
detail: Ukrainian drones struck Russia's Tuapse oil refinery overnight on April 28, the third confirmed hit on the Krasnodar Krai facility in two weeks after earlier strikes destroyed 24 storage tanks and damaged four more. Ukraine's Special Operations Forces also reported a drone strike on an Iskander missile storage facility at a former missile base near Ovrazhki in occupied Crimea, saying the site could support rapid missile attacks on front-line and rear Ukrainian cities.
tn: Tuapse refinery / Ovrazhki missile storage site
dl: HEAVY — Oil-storage fires prolonged; occupied-Crimea missile depot reportedly hit

title: SBU drones strike Perm oil pumping station and Orsk refinery
detail: Oil facilities in Perm Krai and Orsk, Orenburg Oblast, were struck by Ukrainian drones overnight on April 29. The SBU confirmed Alpha unit drones hit a Transneft oil pumping station near Perm, causing a major fire and preliminary reports that nearly all storage tanks were ablaze; local reports also said the Orsknefteorgsintez refinery, a 6.6-million-ton-per-year facility that supplies the Russian army, was hit. Russia's Defense Ministry claimed 98 Ukrainian drones were intercepted across multiple regions and occupied Crimea.
tn: Perm oil pumping station / Orsk refinery
dl: HEAVY — Transneft storage hub burns; Orsk refinery reportedly hit

title: Ukrainian drones strike Russian BARS-Sarmat drone development complex
detail: Ukraine's 413th "Raid" Regiment of the Unmanned Systems Forces reported on April 30 that Ukrainian drones struck the BARS-Sarmat Special Purpose Center in occupied Zaporizhzhia Oblast, a Russian complex used to develop drones, robotic systems, electronic warfare equipment, and command-and-communications gear. The regiment said multiple workshops used to equip and manufacture drones and unmanned ground vehicles were damaged; the exact timing and full extent of the strike were not immediately clear.
tn: Occupied Zaporizhzhia Oblast, Sea of Azov area
dl: HEAVY — Workshops at Russian drone and robotic-systems complex damaged

title: Kyiv proposes long-term ceasefire as US releases $400 million aid package
detail: After Vladimir Putin floated a temporary May 9 Victory Day truce during a call with U.S. President Donald Trump, President Volodymyr Zelensky said Ukraine would push instead for a long-term ceasefire with reliable guarantees. The same day, the Pentagon confirmed it had released a long-delayed $400 million Ukraine Security Assistance Initiative package for procurement of military aid, after months of criticism from U.S. lawmakers over bureaucratic delays.
tn: Kyiv / Washington diplomacy and military aid
dl: LIGHT — Major diplomatic and military-aid developments, no direct battlefield damage

title: Ukraine and European allies launch CORPUS defense-procurement coalition
detail: Ukraine's Defense Procurement Agency launched CORPUS, the Coalition for Resilient Procurement and Unified Support, with procurement agencies from Finland, Italy, Norway, Sweden, and the United Kingdom. Officials said the coalition will begin with information-sharing and coordination between procurement bodies, with joint procurement mechanisms possible later; the launch also comes as Kyiv prepares a framework for limited Ukrainian arms exports, especially high-demand defense technologies such as drone interceptors.
tn: Kyiv and European partner procurement agencies
dl: LIGHT — New allied procurement-coordination channel supports Ukrainian arms production and possible exports

title: Ukraine and European Parliament back international Russia-war reparations commission
detail: Ukraine's parliament ratified the International Claims Commission, becoming the first country to approve the body meant to let victims seek compensation for damage caused by Russia's invasion. The European Parliament also voted for EU support for the claims commission and a special tribunal on the crime of aggression; 25 countries must sign off on the claims commission, while the Kyiv Independent reported that more than 45,000 damage claims have already been submitted to the existing register.
dl: LIGHT — Legal mechanism for Russian war-damage compensation advances in Kyiv and Brussels

title: Ukraine orders 8,000 Octopus interceptor drones to strengthen air defense
detail: Ukraine's Defense Ministry announced it is buying 8,000 Ukrainian-made Octopus interceptor drones for the Defense Forces to expand protection against Shahed-type strike drones. The ministry said the self-guided interceptors have shown consistent effectiveness in combat, can operate day or night at low altitude and under electronic warfare pressure, and are being scaled through 29 licensed Ukrainian companies with UK government participation; four manufacturers have already signed state contracts.
dl: LIGHT — 8,000 interceptor-drone procurement expands Shahed-defense capacity

title: Russian drone attack on Odesa injures at least 20 and damages civilian sites
detail: Russian drones struck Odesa overnight on April 30, hitting residential neighborhoods in several districts and injuring at least 20 civilians, including a 17-year-old boy; local officials said two victims were in intensive care. Fires broke out in damaged high-rise and five-story apartment buildings in Prymorskyi district, while a kindergarten, shopping center, hotel, administrative buildings, infrastructure facilities, warehouses, garages, buses, and passenger cars were also damaged. Ukraine's Air Force said the broader overnight attack involved one ballistic missile and 206 drones, with 172 drones downed or neutralized and 32 drones plus the missile hitting 22 locations.
dl: HEAVY — Residential districts, kindergarten, and infrastructure damaged; at least 20 injured

title: Ukrainian firm unveils Khmarynka mid-range strike drone for mass deployment
detail: General Cherry unveiled Khmarynka, a low-cost mid-range strike drone designed for rapid mass production and battlefield testing by Ukraine's Defense Forces. The fixed-wing system is modeled on Russia's Molniya class, can reportedly carry a 5–8 kg payload up to 50 km, fly for up to 60 minutes, and is intended to exhaust Russian air defenses while striking armored vehicles, warehouses, and infantry bunkers; the company said an initial batch of 150 drones would be provided free for combat feedback.
dl: LIGHT — New low-cost mid-range drone enters Ukrainian mass-strike toolkit

title: Russian strikes fan out across Ukrainian regions after mass drone wave
detail: As Russia's May 1 drone and shelling campaign continued beyond the major Ternopil strike, Ukrainian regional officials reported additional civilian damage and casualties across several oblasts. Dnipropetrovsk Oblast authorities said Russian drones and artillery hit Nikopol, Marhanets, Myrove, Chervonohryhorivka, Pokrovske, and Zelenodolsk nearly 50 times since morning, injuring four people and damaging a medical facility, shops, homes, vehicles, and a utility building. Kherson prosecutors reported artillery, mortar, and drone attacks that killed a 65-year-old cyclist in Kherson and injured five people, while Rivne Oblast reported five injured and damage to residential buildings, vehicles, and civilian infrastructure. Bohodukhiv in Kharkiv Oblast was also hit by a Molniia-type UAV at a food-processing plant, injuring four workers after more than 20 local strikes over 24 hours; Zhytomyr reported damage to sports and educational infrastructure without casualties.
tn: Dnipropetrovsk, Kherson, Rivne, Kharkiv, and Zhytomyr oblasts
dl: HEAVY — Regional drone, artillery, and mortar attacks kill 1 and injure at least 18 outside Ternopil

title: Ukraine says drones hit Su-57s and Su-34 at Shagol airfield
detail: Ukraine's Unmanned Systems Forces and General Staff confirmed that an April 25 long-range drone operation struck Russian combat aircraft at Shagol airfield in Chelyabinsk Oblast, roughly 1,700 km from Ukraine. Follow-up satellite reconnaissance and USF reporting said at least three deep-strike assets hit two Su-57 stealth fighters, one Su-34 fighter-bomber, and another Su-family aircraft of unidentified modification, with varying damage; USF commander Robert Brovdi said targeting Su-34 and Su-57 aircraft is meant to reduce Russia's long-range strike capacity against Ukrainian infrastructure, military sites, and civilians.
dl: HEAVY — Rare confirmed deep strike damages high-value Su-57 and Su-34 aircraft

title: Russia launches mass daytime drone attack centered on Ternopil
detail: Russian forces carried out a mass daytime drone attack across Ukraine on May 1, with Ternopil in western Ukraine taking the brunt of the strike. Local officials reported at least 12 people injured, industrial sites and infrastructure damaged, more than 20 explosions, temporary power outages, and 39 evacuations; the regional military administration said 36 aerial targets were detected over the city and 27 were shot down. Ukraine's General Staff said the wider daytime assault involved 409 drones from 8 a.m. to 3:30 p.m., with 388 shot down and 16 impacts recorded.
dl: HEAVY — 409-drone daytime attack injures civilians and damages infrastructure

title: US envoys' Kyiv visit stalls as Ukraine-Russia peace talks remain frozen
detail: The Kyiv Independent reported that U.S. President Donald Trump's envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner are not rushing to visit Kyiv, despite months of discussion about a trip meant to restart trilateral diplomacy between Ukraine, Russia, and the United States. Ukrainian officials said talks have been frozen for over two months, Washington's focus has shifted toward Iran, and Russia continues demanding Ukrainian withdrawal from Kyiv-held parts of Donbas while Ukraine insists a ceasefire should be based on freezing the current front line; a U.S. official said the visit remains under discussion but is not confirmed.
dl: LIGHT — Diplomatic reset delayed as peace-talk format remains stalled

title: Zelensky announces major army pay and service-term reform
detail: President Volodymyr Zelensky announced a major reform of Ukraine's armed forces focused on higher pay and clearer recruitment and service rules, with first changes planned for June. Zelensky said rear-position minimum pay should rise to at least Hr 30,000 while infantry pay should range from Hr 250,000 to Hr 400,000 depending on combat tasks, experience, and effectiveness; the reform is also intended to create predictable service terms and conditions for phased discharge of previously mobilized soldiers starting this year.
dl: LIGHT — Major manpower and service-policy reform announced

title: Zelensky says Ukraine's oil-infrastructure strike campaign cost Russia $7 billion in 2026
detail: President Volodymyr Zelensky said Ukrainian long-range attacks on Russian oil infrastructure had cost Moscow at least $7 billion since the start of 2026, citing direct damage, prolonged downtime, and disrupted shipments. He said April marked a "new level" in the campaign and that Ukraine was preparing decisions to further expand long-range strike capabilities; Bloomberg data cited by the Kyiv Independent recorded at least 21 April strikes on refineries, pipelines, and maritime assets, pushing Russian refinery throughput to its lowest level since December 2009.
dl: HEAVY — Strategic oil-strike campaign reportedly cuts billions from Russian revenue

title: US 2027 defense budget proposal excludes Ukraine military aid, senator says
detail: Independent Senator Angus King said President Donald Trump's proposed 2027 U.S. defense budget contains no military assistance for Ukraine. At a Senate Armed Services Committee hearing, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth argued that European countries should lead funding because the threat is closer to them, while King warned that the United States had "stepped back" from an existential struggle for democracy as Russia benefits from weakened sanctions and higher oil revenues during the U.S.-Iran war.
dl: MODERATE — Proposed U.S. defense budget omits Ukraine military aid as Europe is pressed to pay more

title: Zelensky orders added air-defense measures for Dnipro and Odesa after dense drone attacks
detail: President Volodymyr Zelensky said Ukraine would reinforce air defenses after Russia launched more than 400 drones during the day, including over 200 Shaheds, and still achieved hits despite Ukraine reporting 388 interceptions. Zelensky said military command and the Defense Ministry had already approved additional radars, electronic-warfare systems, and crews for Dnipro, while Odesa would also receive measures to push its interception rate higher; he said Kyiv is working daily with partners on components and will continue seeking May contributions through the PURL initiative and other defense-cooperation channels.
dl: LIGHT — New radars, EW systems, and crews ordered after high-density drone barrage

title: Ukraine confirms Perm refinery damage and Buk-M3 strike in broader rear-area attack wave
detail: Ukraine's General Staff said Defense Forces struck Russian military-economic and logistics targets around May 1, including renewed hits on Tuapse, confirmed damage to the AVT-4 primary oil-processing unit at the Perm refinery with a follow-on fire, and a Buk-M3 surface-to-air missile system near occupied Olkhovatka in Donetsk Oblast. The same strike wave also targeted ammunition depots near Rozdolne and Rovenky, a logistics warehouse near Melitopol, a UAV warehouse near Dalne in Belgorod Oblast, UAV control points in Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk sectors, and a command-observation post near Kairy in Kherson Oblast.
tn: Perm refinery and Russian rear-area support targets
dl: HEAVY — Refinery processing unit, air-defense asset, depots, and command/logistics nodes targeted

title: Ukrainian drones hammer Tuapse oil refinery for fourth time in a month
detail: SBU Alpha, military intelligence, and the Unmanned Systems Forces struck the Tuapse oil refinery and marine terminal overnight on May 1, the fourth confirmed Ukrainian attack on the Black Sea Coast facility in a month. The strike ignited oil tanks and sent a column of black smoke above the site shortly after Russian emergency officials claimed earlier fires had been extinguished; prior attacks had destroyed 24 tanks, damaged four more, and hit crude-processing infrastructure, while Bloomberg-linked reporting said April strikes pushed Russian refinery capacity to its lowest level since 2009.
tn: Tuapse oil refinery and marine terminal
dl: HEAVY — Fourth strike reignites tanks at hard-hit Black Sea refinery hub

title: ISW assesses Russia suffered first monthly net territorial loss since Kursk incursion
detail: The Institute for the Study of War assessed that Russian forces seized or infiltrated 28.28 square kilometers in April 2026 but suffered a net loss of 116 square kilometers overall, the first monthly reversal since Ukraine's Kursk incursion in August 2024. ISW said the pace of Russian advances had continued declining since late 2025 even as Moscow sustained offensive pressure across several sectors.
dl: MODERATE — First monthly net Russian territorial loss signals slowing offensive momentum

title: Sweden seizes Russian shadow-fleet tanker amid wider pressure on Moscow oil logistics
detail: Swedish authorities seized a Russian shadow-fleet oil tanker on May 3 as European states increased pressure on Moscow's maritime oil logistics. The move came alongside continuing Ukrainian strikes on Russian refineries and sea-drone activity around Black Sea tanker routes, keeping Russia's export system under military and legal pressure.
dl: MODERATE — European enforcement adds pressure to Russian oil-shipping network

title: Russia launches missiles and hundreds of drones in renewed mass attack wave
detail: Russian forces launched two Iskander-M ballistic missiles, five Kh-59/69 cruise missiles, and 234 drones toward Ukraine during the day on May 3, followed by another 155 drones overnight into May 4. ISW and Ukrainian reporting framed the barrage as part of Russia's continuing effort to saturate Ukrainian air defenses with mixed missile, Shahed-type strike drone, and decoy-drone packages.
dl: HEAVY — Multi-wave missile and drone barrage strains air defenses

title: Russia appoints Alexander Chayko to command Aerospace Forces after air-defense criticism
detail: Russia's Defense Ministry appointed Colonel General Alexander Chayko as commander-in-chief of the Russian Aerospace Forces following domestic criticism of Russian air defense and air-campaign failures. The personnel move came as Ukraine continued deep-strike pressure against air bases, refineries, and military-industrial sites inside Russia.
dl: LIGHT — Senior Russian air-command reshuffle follows sustained strike pressure

title: Putin and Zelensky trade rival ceasefire proposals around Victory Day
detail: Russia announced a May 8–9 ceasefire for Victory Day commemorations, while President Volodymyr Zelensky rejected the short holiday truce as cynical and proposed an earlier pause beginning the night of May 5–6. The competing declarations left fighting unresolved and underscored Kyiv's demand for a broader, verifiable ceasefire rather than a symbolic two-day pause.
dl: LIGHT — Rival ceasefire proposals create diplomatic pressure but no verified pause

title: Russian fiber-optic FPV drones appear deeper inside Kramatorsk
detail: The Kyiv Independent reported that Russian fiber-optic FPV drones, which are harder to jam than radio-controlled models, were appearing deeper inside Kramatorsk. The development threatened Ukraine's fortified Donbas logistics and civilian hub by extending precision-drone reach into areas previously less exposed to short-range FPV strikes.
dl: MODERATE — Jam-resistant FPV threat pushes deeper into Donbas stronghold

title: Ukrainian Flamingo missiles strike Russian military-industrial site in Cheboksary
detail: Ukrainian F-5 Flamingo cruise missiles reportedly struck military-industrial facilities in Cheboksary, Chuvashia, more than 1,500 kilometers from Ukraine. Reporting said the target area produced navigation components and other equipment for Russia's military, while regional authorities acknowledged casualties from a Ukrainian drone or missile attack.
dl: HEAVY — Deep strike hits defense-industrial node over 1,500 km from Ukraine

title: Ukraine drone strike forces halt at Kirishi oil refinery
detail: Ukrainian long-range drones struck the Kirishinefteorgsintez refinery in Russia's Leningrad region, causing a fire and reportedly forcing production to stop at one of Russia's largest oil-processing sites. The attack extended Kyiv's 2026 campaign against Russian fuel infrastructure after earlier strikes on Tuapse, Perm, and other refinery targets.
dl: HEAVY — Major Russian refinery reportedly stops production after drone fire

title: Russian strikes kill at least 22 across Ukraine before Kyiv ceasefire deadline
detail: Russian missile, drone, and glide-bomb attacks killed at least 22 people and wounded dozens more across Ukraine on May 5, including reported deaths in Zaporizhzhia, Kramatorsk, and at Naftogaz gas-production facilities in Poltava Oblast. Ukrainian officials said Russia used ballistic missiles, cruise missiles, Shahed-type drones, and double-tap tactics against emergency workers even as ceasefire proposals approached.
tn: Zaporizhzhia, Kramatorsk, Poltava, and wider Ukraine
dl: CRITICAL — At least 22 killed and 80+ wounded in widespread pre-ceasefire strikes

title: Russia violates Ukraine's May 6 ceasefire with missiles, drones, and glide bombs
detail: Hours after Kyiv's unilateral ceasefire was due to begin at midnight on May 6, Ukrainian officials said Russian attacks continued across the north, east, and south. Russia launched two Iskander-M ballistic missiles, one Kh-31 missile, and 108 drones from the evening of May 5 into the morning of May 6; Ukrainian air defenses reported shooting down or suppressing 89 drones. Strikes hit Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, Kryvyi Rih, Sumy, Dnipropetrovsk, Kherson, and other frontline areas, killing at least one civilian in Sumy and wounding several others while damaging homes, industrial infrastructure, and other facilities.
tn: Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, Sumy, Kryvyi Rih, and wider Ukraine
dl: HEAVY — Missile, drone, and glide-bomb attacks continue despite Kyiv's ceasefire start

title: Ukrainian drone attack hits Dzhankoi in occupied Crimea, Russian-installed officials say five killed
detail: Russian-installed authorities in occupied Crimea said a Ukrainian drone attack struck Dzhankoi, a northern Crimean town that hosts a military airbase, late on May 5 into May 6. The Russian-appointed governor claimed five civilians were killed, while Russia's Defense Ministry said it intercepted 53 Ukrainian drones over Russian regions, occupied Crimea, and the Black Sea during the wider overnight barrage. The reported strike added to pressure around Moscow's Victory Day ceasefire claims and Ukraine's expanding deep-strike campaign.
dl: MODERATE — Strike near Crimean airbase reported to cause five deaths amid wider drone barrage

title: ISW reports opposing frontline shifts in Sumy and Zaporizhia amid ceasefire failure
detail: ISW assessed that Russian forces recently advanced in northern Sumy Oblast, likely seizing Sopych and infiltrating west of the settlement, while Ukrainian forces made marginal gains in western Zaporizhia Oblast. The limited shifts came as both sides continued counterattacks and infiltration missions despite competing unilateral ceasefire declarations around Russia's Victory Day period.
tn: Sopych, Sumy Oblast and western Zaporizhia front
dl: MODERATE — Localized but confirmed frontline movement on northern and southern axes

title: Russian overnight drone wave kills at least 13 and wounds 54 across Ukraine
detail: Russian forces launched 102 Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas, Parodiya, and other drones overnight into May 7 from multiple Russian and occupied-territory launch areas. Ukraine reported shooting down or suppressing 92 drones, while eight strike drones hit six locations. Regional authorities reported at least 13 civilians killed and 54 injured across Donetsk, Sumy, Zaporizhzhia, Kherson, Dnipropetrovsk, and Kharkiv oblasts.
dl: CRITICAL — Nationwide drone wave causes double-digit deaths and dozens wounded

title: Ukraine launches one of its largest drone attacks on Russia; Moscow Oblast logistics site reportedly hit
detail: Russia's Defense Ministry claimed air defenses downed 347 Ukrainian drones over more than 20 regions, including Moscow, in what AP described as Ukraine's second-largest aerial attack of the full-scale war. During the same overnight wave, Ukrainian drones reportedly struck the Nara production and logistics complex in Naro-Fominsk, a Russian Defense Ministry facility used to store and distribute military supplies southwest of Moscow. The attack underscored Kyiv's expanding deep-strike reach ahead of Russia's Victory Day ceremonies.
tn: Nara logistics complex, Naro-Fominsk, Moscow Oblast
dl: HEAVY — Large-scale deep-strike wave pressures Moscow-area military logistics and air defenses

title: Russia tells foreign embassies in Kyiv to evacuate ahead of Victory Day strike threats
detail: Russia warned foreign diplomatic missions and international organizations in Kyiv to evacuate personnel, saying Moscow could conduct retaliatory strikes, including against 'decision-making centers,' if Ukraine disrupted Russia's May 9 Victory Day ceremonies. The warning followed Moscow's rejection of Kyiv's earlier ceasefire proposal and escalating reciprocal drone and missile activity around the holiday period.
dl: MODERATE — Diplomatic evacuation warning raises risk around threatened Kyiv strikes

title: Russia's Victory Day ceasefire collapses within hours amid continued strikes on Ukraine
detail: Russia's unilateral May 8-10 Victory Day ceasefire appeared to collapse almost immediately. President Volodymyr Zelensky said there was "not even a token attempt" at a ceasefire, reporting more than 850 Russian drone strikes of various types since midnight, over 140 strikes on frontline positions, and 10 Russian assaults overnight, mostly in the Slovyansk sector. Ukraine's Air Force said Russia launched 67 drones overnight and that 56 were neutralized, while Dnipropetrovsk officials reported at least 30 attacks by drones, artillery, and a missile that injured three people and damaged homes and vehicles.
dl: HEAVY — Ceasefire claim collapses as Russia continues large-scale drones, shelling, and frontline assaults

title: Ukrainian drone wave hits Yaroslavl refinery and Rostov defense-linked sites as Russia reports 264 drones downed
detail: Ukrainian long-range strikes hit multiple deep-rear targets in Russia just after Moscow's declared Victory Day ceasefire began. Russian officials said air defenses destroyed 264 Ukrainian fixed-wing drones between midnight and 7 a.m.; Moscow's mayor said 27 drones headed for the capital were shot down. Russian and Ukrainian reporting indicated fires at the Slavneft-YANOS oil refinery in Yaroslavl, one of Russia's five largest refineries and roughly 700 kilometers from Ukraine, and explosions near Rostov-on-Don industrial sites including the Radar Scientific and Technical Center, a defense-linked radar developer, and the Empils chemical plant area.
tn: Slavneft-YANOS refinery, Yaroslavl; Rostov-on-Don industrial zone
dl: CRITICAL — Deep-strike wave hits major refinery and defense-industrial targets hundreds of kilometers inside Russia

title: Drone strike on Rostov air traffic control center paralyzes more than a dozen southern Russian airports
detail: Russia's Transportation Ministry said a Ukrainian drone crashed into the Rostov-on-Don regional air traffic control center, damaging equipment and forcing the center to suspend work. More than a dozen airports across southern Russia, including Sochi, Volgograd, Krasnodar, Grozny, Mineralnye Vody, Astrakhan, Stavropol, and others, halted operations; Russian airlines adjusted schedules and canceled flights, while the Association of Tour Operators of Russia said at least 14,000 passengers were stuck by the disruptions. No injuries were reported.
dl: HEAVY — Strike disables regional air traffic control and disrupts aviation across southern Russia

title: Azov Corps drones interdict Russian logistics around occupied Mariupol up to 160 km behind the front
detail: Ukraine's 1st Azov National Guard Corps released footage and said its reconnaissance and strike drones were operating over occupied Mariupol and patrolling roads up to 160 kilometers behind the front line. ISW assessed the activity as part of an intensifying Ukrainian mid-range strike campaign against Russian ground lines of communication, noting geolocated strikes along the T-0509 Mariupol-Donetsk highway roughly 95 kilometers from the front and drones operating near the M-14 route through Mariupol. The routes support Russian logistics toward northern Donetsk, Zaporizhzhia, Kherson, and the Orikhiv direction.
dl: HEAVY — Ukrainian mid-range drones threaten Russian operational logistics around occupied Mariupol

sources.name: Institute for the Study of War

title: SBU says drones hit Lukoil's Perm refinery and a Transneft pumping station for third time in two weeks
detail: Ukraine's SBU said its Alpha special operations unit struck the Lukoil-Permnefteorgsintez refinery and the Perm linear production dispatch station more than 1,500 kilometers from Ukraine. The SBU said a fire broke out at a crude oil processing unit and that one oil reservoir at the Transneft-operated pumping station was hit. The attack was reported as the third strike on Perm oil infrastructure in roughly two weeks, extending Kyiv's deep-strike campaign against Russian refining, fuel supply, and oil transport nodes.
tn: Lukoil-Permnefteorgsintez refinery and Perm oil pumping station
dl: CRITICAL — Deep strike hits major refinery and oil transport hub more than 1,500 km from Ukraine

title: Russia and Ukraine confirm US-brokered May 9-11 ceasefire and 1,000-for-1,000 POW exchange
detail: U.S. President Donald Trump announced that Russia and Ukraine agreed to a three-day ceasefire from May 9 to May 11, suspending kinetic activity around Russia's Victory Day period and pairing the pause with a planned exchange of 1,000 prisoners from each side. President Volodymyr Zelensky confirmed Russia's agreement to the 1,000-for-1,000 format and said Ukraine expected Washington to ensure Moscow fulfilled the deal, while Kremlin aide Yuri Ushakov confirmed Russia accepted the initiative. The agreement followed several days of failed unilateral ceasefire proposals and threats around Moscow's Victory Day parade.
tn: Kyiv-Moscow ceasefire and prisoner exchange talks
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Temporary truce and large POW exchange create first short pause after failed unilateral ceasefires

sources.name: Institute for the Study of War

title: May 9 ceasefire largely holds overnight despite pre-midnight drone launches and continued uncertainty
detail: The temporary May 9-11 ceasefire appeared to remain intact overnight into Russia's Victory Day parade, with no major reports of attacks after midnight and one of Ukraine's longest periods without a nationwide air raid alert since the full-scale invasion began. Ukraine's Air Force said Russia launched 43 drones overnight and downed 34, but it was unclear whether the launches occurred before or after the truce began; Russia said it intercepted 44 Ukrainian drones between 8 p.m. and midnight Moscow time on May 8. Zelensky also issued a deliberately pointed decree excluding Red Square from Ukrainian long-range strikes during the parade, prioritizing the prisoner exchange over a strike on the event.
dl: LOW — Temporary truce mostly holds overnight despite residual drone activity around midnight

title: Three-day ceasefire frays as Russian strikes kill 1 and injure 19 across Ukraine
detail: The U.S.-brokered May 9-11 ceasefire deteriorated on its first day as regional authorities reported Russian attacks across Ukraine killed at least one civilian and injured at least 19 over the prior day. Ukraine's Air Force said it intercepted all 27 Russian drones launched overnight from Primorsko-Akhtarsk and Millerovo, while officials reported injuries in Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk, Donetsk, Kherson, Zaporizhzhia, and Sumy oblasts; Zaporizhzhia authorities said Russian forces carried out 780 strikes on 33 settlements, and Ukraine's General Staff reported 147 combat clashes despite the truce.
dl: HEAVY — Declared truce breaks down with nationwide strikes, civilian casualties, and continued frontline clashes

sources.name: Institute for the Study of War

title: Russian drone hits Kharkiv apartment block less than 24 hours into ceasefire
detail: A Russian drone struck a nine-story residential building in Kharkiv's Industrial district late on May 9, less than a day into the declared May 9-11 truce. Kharkiv Oblast officials said civilians were affected, including two eight-year-old boys who suffered acute stress reactions, while emergency crews worked at the site. The strike underscored that the pause had not stopped attacks on civilian areas even as Kyiv had refrained from disrupting Moscow's Victory Day parade.
dl: MODERATE — Drone hits residential high-rise during declared ceasefire, causing civilian trauma and damage

title: ISW says both sides kept up limited operations while Russia used ceasefire for logistics and rotations
detail: ISW assessed that Russian and Ukrainian forces continued limited offensive operations on May 9 despite the declared ceasefire, though at reduced intensity. The assessment said Russian forces used the pause for rotations, reinforcements, redeployments, and logistics likely meant to support future offensive operations, while Ukrainian forces recently advanced in northern Kharkiv Oblast and the Borova direction and Russia reported or claimed continued activity in several sectors. ISW also noted that the planned 1,000-for-1,000 prisoner exchange had not yet taken place by the end of May 9.
dl: MODERATE — Ceasefire reduces but does not stop operations as Russia repositions and resupplies

sources.name: Institute for the Study of War

title: US-brokered ceasefire comes under serious strain as Russia continues frontline attacks and drone strikes
detail: On the second day of the May 9-11 U.S.-mediated ceasefire, Ukrainian officials said Russia kept up assaults and drone attacks despite avoiding the largest missile-and-drone barrages. Reuters reported three people killed by Russian drone strikes in Zaporizhzhia, Dnipropetrovsk, and Kherson oblasts, eight wounded in Kharkiv Oblast, seven wounded in Kherson Oblast, and a rescue driver wounded in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast. Ukraine's General Staff reported nearly 210 combat clashes since early May 9, while Zelensky said Russian forces were not observing silence on the front; Russia accused Ukraine of violations and said it downed 57 Ukrainian drones.
dl: HEAVY — Ceasefire effectively frays with civilian deaths, wounded residents, and hundreds of frontline clashes

title: Latvian defense minister resigns after diverted Ukrainian drones strike oil tanks in Rezekne
detail: Latvian Defense Minister Andris Spruds resigned after two Ukrainian drones, reportedly diverted from targets in Russia by Russian electronic warfare, crossed from Russia into Latvia and struck oil storage facilities in Rezekne on May 7. Prime Minister Evika Silina said anti-drone systems had not been deployed fast enough and appointed army colonel Raivis Melnis as the new defense minister. Latvia and Lithuania called for stronger NATO air defenses, while Ukraine said it was prepared to send specialists to help the Baltic states and Finland prevent similar incidents.
dl: MODERATE — Drone spillover into NATO territory causes oil-site damage and a Latvian defense leadership shake-up

title: Ukraine submits list for 1,000-for-1,000 POW exchange and presses US to guarantee deal
detail: President Volodymyr Zelensky said Ukraine had handed Russia a list of 1,000 prisoners for the planned 1,000-for-1,000 exchange linked to the May 9-11 ceasefire arrangement. Zelensky said the exchange was being prepared, must take place, and that Kyiv expected the United States to play an active guarantor role because Washington mediated the arrangement. The announcement kept the largest proposed prisoner swap of the current U.S.-led peace push alive even as the battlefield ceasefire was breaking down.
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Kyiv advances the planned 1,000-for-1,000 POW swap despite ceasefire violations

title: May 9-11 ceasefire expires with 180 clashes and renewed accusations of violations
detail: As the U.S.-mediated May 9-11 ceasefire drew to a close, President Volodymyr Zelensky said there had been no silence on the front line and that Russia had no intention of ending the war. Reuters reported that both sides accused the other of drone, artillery, and battlefield violations; Ukraine's General Staff recorded 180 clashes over the previous 24 hours and 38 additional Russian assaults by Monday afternoon, while Ukrainian regional officials reported at least three civilians killed in Zaporizhzhia and Kherson oblasts. Russia's Defense Ministry claimed 23,802 Ukrainian ceasefire violations and said Russian troops responded in kind, while Belgorod officials said one person was killed and three wounded by a Ukrainian attack.
dl: SIGNIFICANT — U.S.-brokered ceasefire ends with large-scale reported violations, 180 clashes, and civilian deaths

title: Zelensky offers deep-strike ceasefire as Germany and Ukraine deepen long-range drone cooperation
detail: President Volodymyr Zelensky said Ukraine had refrained from long-range strikes while Russia avoided mass missile-and-drone attacks, and offered to keep holding back those 'long-range sanctions' if Moscow maintained the same restraint. He warned that if Russia returned to full-scale bombardment, Ukraine's response would be immediate and tangible. The same day, German Defense Minister Boris Pistorius and Ukrainian Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov signed a letter of intent in Kyiv to deepen defense-tech cooperation, including Brave Germany grants, support for interceptor drones and air defense, and joint development of drones with ranges up to 1,500 kilometers.
tn: Kyiv defense cooperation and long-range strike policy
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Kyiv links long-range strikes to Russian mass-attack restraint while Berlin expands drone and air-defense cooperation

title: ISW highlights Ukrainian drone pressure on Russian Sea of Azov logistics near Mariupol
detail: ISW assessed that Ukrainian drone operations near occupied Mariupol were alarming Russian milbloggers because Starlink-enabled strike drones reportedly could reach up to 200 kilometers behind the line. The concern centered on the M-14 Mariupol-Berdyansk-Melitopol-Henichesk highway and other roads along the Sea of Azov coast, critical Russian ground lines of communication linking occupied Donetsk Oblast to Crimea. ISW framed the activity as part of an expanding Ukrainian battlefield air-interdiction campaign against Russian logistics, even as both sides maintained only limited offensive operations during the final day of the ceasefire.
tn: M-14 Sea of Azov logistics corridor near occupied Mariupol
dl: MODERATE — Ukrainian medium-range drones threaten key Russian logistics corridor to occupied Crimea

sources.name: Institute for the Study of War

title: Russia launches more than 200 drones hours after ceasefire ends, hitting Kyiv homes and Mykolaiv energy sites
detail: Hours after the May 9-11 ceasefire expired, Russia launched 216 drones at Ukraine, with Ukrainian air defenses downing or neutralizing 192, according to Ukraine's Air Force. Foreign Minister Andrii Sybiha said Kyiv had proposed extending the partial truce but Moscow escalated instead, targeting civilian infrastructure including a kindergarten and causing at least one death and six injuries. Kyiv officials reported drone debris setting fire to the roof of a 16-storey residential building in Obolon, while Kyiv Oblast officials said a kindergarten roof burned in Fastiv. Officials also reported damage in Cherkasy, Zhytomyr, Chernihiv, Dnipro, and Kherson, and Mykolaiv Governor Vitaliy Kim said Russian drones hit energy infrastructure and caused blackouts in several settlements.
tn: Kyiv, Mykolaiv, and multiple Ukrainian regions
dl: MAJOR — Post-ceasefire drone barrage damages homes, kindergarten, transport, and energy infrastructure across Ukraine

title: Russian attacks across Dnipropetrovsk region kill eight after ceasefire expires
detail: Russian forces carried out nearly 30 strikes across Dnipropetrovsk Oblast after the May 9-11 ceasefire expired, killing eight civilians and wounding 11, according to regional officials. The attacks hit Nikopol and nearby communities, Dubovyky and Mykolaivka in Synelnykove district, and Kryvyi Rih, where a drone struck a residential building, killed two people, wounded four, and left a nine-month-old girl critically injured. Officials reported damage to industrial facilities, infrastructure, a gas pipeline, more than 20 homes, private houses, and vehicles.
tn: Dnipropetrovsk region, including Kryvyi Rih, Nikopol, and Synelnykove districts
dl: MAJOR — Regional post-ceasefire strikes kill 8 civilians, wound 11, and damage homes, industry, infrastructure, and gas lines

title: Ukraine says it hit Russian gas and defense-industry targets more than 1,500 km from the border
detail: President Volodymyr Zelensky said Ukraine had carried out long-range 'mirror response' strikes against Russian gas-industry facilities in Orenburg Oblast, more than 1,500 kilometers from Ukraine, after renewed Russian drone and bomb attacks. ISW reported that geolocated footage showed likely Ukrainian drone debris in Orenburg and that Russian opposition outlet Astra said the drones may have targeted JSC Strela Production Association, a defense-industrial enterprise that produces supersonic anti-ship missiles and components for fighter aircraft. Reuters and Al Jazeera also reported Zelensky's statement that Ukrainian forces struck gas facilities in Orenburg as retaliation for Russia's overnight attacks.
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Ukrainian long-range strike campaign reaches Orenburg, over 1,500 km from Ukraine

sources.name: Institute for the Study of War

title: ISW reports Ukrainian advances near Slovyansk and Kostyantynivka as Russia's offensive stalls
detail: ISW assessed that Ukrainian forces recently advanced in central Riznykivka east of Slovyansk and in the Kostyantynivka-Druzhkivka tactical area, with geolocated footage indicating Ukrainian positions east and west of Kostyantynivka where Russian sources had previously claimed Russian positions. ISW said Russian forces were still conducting infiltration missions and heavy FPV-drone interdiction around Slovyansk, Kramatorsk, and Kostyantynivka, but that Russia's spring-summer offensive had failed to produce operationally significant advances. ISW also assessed that Russian forces suffered a net loss of territory across the theater in April 2026 for the first time since Ukraine's 2024 Kursk incursion.
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Geolocated advances and ISW assessment point to continued Russian offensive underperformance

sources.name: Institute for the Study of War

title: Russia fires 139 drones in continuing post-ceasefire wave, damaging Odesa and Kharkiv infrastructure
detail: Russia launched 139 Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas, and decoy drones from Kursk, Bryansk, Millerovo, Primorsko-Akhtarsk, and occupied Crimea starting on the evening of May 12, Ukraine's Air Force said. Ukrainian defenses shot down or neutralized 111 drones by 8:00 a.m. on May 13, but hits by 20 UAVs were recorded at 13 locations and the attack was still ongoing with dozens of drones in the air. Odesa regional officials reported multiple waves against industrial facilities that damaged warehouses and utility buildings without casualties, while Kharkiv officials said strikes hit the Shevchenkivskyi and Kholodnohirskyi districts, including an infrastructure facility that caught fire.
tn: Odesa and Kharkiv infrastructure targets amid nationwide drone attack
dl: MAJOR — Second consecutive post-ceasefire drone barrage records 20 hits, damaging industrial and infrastructure sites

title: Ukraine hits Russian oil and gas infrastructure from the Black Sea to the Caspian
detail: Ukraine's General Staff said Ukrainian forces struck the Tamanneftegaz oil terminal near the Black Sea port of Taman, primary oil-refining units at the Yaroslavl oil refinery, and infrastructure at the Astrakhan gas processing plant overnight on May 13, with fires reported at multiple sites. Kyiv Independent reported NASA FIRMS heat anomalies near Tamanneftegaz and said the terminal transships crude oil, fuel oil, diesel, and LPG, while ISW assessed the strikes as part of a widening Ukrainian campaign against Russian oil infrastructure and military logistics, including recent strikes as far as 1,500 kilometers from Ukraine.
tn: Tamanneftegaz terminal, Yaroslavl oil refinery, and Astrakhan gas processing plant
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Ukrainian long-range strike wave hits multiple Russian energy export and processing facilities

sources.name: Institute for the Study of War

title: Russia launches prolonged daytime drone assault on western Ukraine, killing at least six
detail: Russia fired hundreds of drones in waves through May 13, with President Volodymyr Zelensky saying at least 800 drones had been launched since midnight and Ukraine's Air Force later reporting 753 UAVs between 08:00 and 18:30, of which 710 were neutralized. Reuters reported that the attack targeted critical infrastructure in western regions near NATO borders, killed at least six people and wounded dozens, damaged Naftogaz facilities in Kharkiv and Zhytomyr regions, struck railway infrastructure 23 times, hit Ivano-Frankivsk housing, and cut power in Zhovkva. Poland scrambled fighters, Slovakia temporarily closed border crossings, Moldova reported a drone crossing its airspace, and Hungary summoned Russia's ambassador over attacks on areas with ethnic Hungarian communities.
tn: Western and central Ukraine, including Rivne, Ivano-Frankivsk, Lviv/Zhovkva, Zhytomyr, Kharkiv, and railway infrastructure
dl: MAJOR — Prolonged drone assault targets western Ukrainian critical infrastructure and triggers NATO-border responses

sources.name: Institute for the Study of War

title: Kremlin hardens territorial demands before negotiations despite stalled battlefield gains
detail: ISW assessed that the Kremlin was doubling down on demands that Ukraine cease fire and withdraw from Russian-claimed Ukrainian oblasts as a precondition for renewed peace talks, after spokesman Dmitry Peskov said Ukraine must leave territories Moscow claims as its own. ISW cited Financial Times reporting that Putin may add further territorial demands in any future ceasefire, while noting that Russia's ambitions run counter to battlefield reality: ISW said Russian forces had advanced only 349.89 square kilometers in Donetsk Oblast since the start of 2026 and was no longer prepared to forecast whether Russia could seize the remaining Ukrainian-held Donetsk terrain at all. U.S. President Donald Trump said he had not agreed with Putin that Russia should acquire all of Donetsk and Luhansk.
tn: Moscow negotiation demands and Donbas battlefield reality
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Moscow renews maximalist territorial preconditions as ISW says battlefield gains do not support them

sources.name: Institute for the Study of War

title: Russia fires 675 drones and 56 missiles in largest wartime aerial assault, Kyiv main target
detail: Russia followed the May 13 daytime drone waves with a combined overnight missile-and-drone barrage that Ukrainian officials described as the largest aerial attack over a two-day period since the full-scale war began. Ukraine's Air Force tracked 731 aerial threats, including 675 UAVs and 56 missiles, and said defenses shot down or suppressed 652 drones and 41 missiles, while 15 missiles and 23 drones hit 24 locations. Reuters and Ukrainian officials said Kyiv was the main target: at least three people were killed and about 40 wounded, an apartment building section collapsed, water supplies were disrupted, and damage was recorded at 20 locations in the city and nearby region. Officials also reported disruptions to electricity in 11 regions, strikes on Kremenchuk energy infrastructure, Odesa port infrastructure, and railway facilities, while morning drone strikes in Kharkiv injured 20 people.
tn: Kyiv and nationwide Ukrainian energy, port, railway, and urban infrastructure
dl: MAJOR — Largest two-day aerial assault of the war hits Kyiv, energy, port, rail, and urban infrastructure

title: Kyiv death toll rises as rescuers clear rubble from Russia's largest two-day air assault
detail: Ukrainian emergency officials said the death toll from Russia's May 14 strike on Kyiv rose to at least 21, including three children, while rescue teams continued round-the-clock debris removal at a collapsed residential building in the Darnytskyi district. Kyiv Post, citing later emergency-service updates, reported 24 killed and 47 wounded in Kyiv, with dog teams searching 2,800 square meters and more than 3,000 cubic meters of rubble cleared. Reuters said Russia launched 1,567 drones over two days and at least 27 civilians were killed across Ukraine, while President Volodymyr Zelensky said preliminary analysis indicated a recently manufactured Kh-101 cruise missile hit the apartment block, underscoring continuing Russian access to foreign components.
tn: Kyiv Darnytskyi district residential building and citywide rescue operations
dl: MAJOR — Casualty toll rises sharply after Russian missile collapses Kyiv apartment block during record two-day barrage

title: Ukrainian drone wave hits Ryazan oil refinery and reported military airfield targets inside Russia
detail: Ukrainian drones struck the Ryazan oil refinery, one of Russia's largest oil-processing plants, overnight on May 14-15, with local reports and videos showing repeated explosions, a major fire, and thick smoke above the refinery. Kyiv Independent reported the refinery produces about 17.1 million tons of oil per year and has been repeatedly targeted because of its role in supplying Russia's war machine; Russian officials claimed debris damaged an enterprise and residential buildings, killing three people and injuring 12, while Russia's Defense Ministry said 355 Ukrainian drones were downed overnight. Ukrainska Pravda also reported explosions in Bryansk, Taganrog, and Yeysk, where smoke was seen rising from an air base housing the 859th Naval Aviation Combat Application and Requalification Center, and temporary restrictions at several Russian airports including Moscow Vnukovo.
tn: Ryazan oil refinery, Yeysk air base, and multiple Russian regions
dl: MAJOR — Large Ukrainian long-range drone attack sets major Russian refinery ablaze and disrupts military/airport infrastructure

title: Russia and Ukraine exchange 205 prisoners each in first stage of planned 1,000-for-1,000 swap
detail: Russia and Ukraine each returned 205 prisoners of war on May 15 in what President Volodymyr Zelensky described as the first stage of a larger 1,000-for-1,000 exchange. Reuters reported the swap followed the U.S.-brokered three-day Victory Day ceasefire framework, while The Moscow Times said Russia's Defense Ministry confirmed its freed servicemen were transferred to Belarus for medical and psychological checks and credited the United Arab Emirates with humanitarian assistance during the return. Zelensky said the freed Ukrainian personnel included Armed Forces, National Guard, and State Border Guard Service members who had been in Russian captivity since 2022.
tn: Ukraine prisoner return and Russia-Belarus transfer route
dl: SIGNIFICANT — First tranche of U.S.-brokered 1,000-for-1,000 POW exchange completed despite renewed escalation

title: UN Security Council schedules emergency meeting over escalating Russian attacks on Ukrainian cities
detail: The UN Security Council secretariat told Ukrinform that an urgent meeting on Russia's escalating strikes against Ukrainian civilians and critical infrastructure would be held on May 19 at Ukraine's request. Ukraine's UN ambassador Andrii Melnyk asked China, which held the Council presidency, to convene the session after Russian attacks between May 4 and May 11 used about 600 strike drones and 16 ballistic missiles, killing more than 40 civilians and wounding over 200, followed by the May 13 wave of more than 800 drones. Melnyk argued the strikes showed Russia choosing escalation and terror over diplomacy and said systematic attacks on civilians violated international humanitarian law and could amount to war crimes and crimes against humanity.
tn: United Nations Security Council, New York
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Ukraine secures emergency UNSC session over renewed Russian mass-strike campaign against civilians and infrastructure

sources.name: Institute for the Study of War

title: Russian drones strike marked UN humanitarian convoy in Kherson's Ostrov district
detail: A UN inter-agency humanitarian mission led by OCHA was struck by drones while delivering aid in the Ukrainian-controlled Ostrov neighborhood of Kherson on May 14, with UN News reporting the vehicle had been notified to the parties in advance and the UN Secretary-General stressing that humanitarian relief personnel and objects must be protected under international humanitarian law. Militarnyi reported that Russian Telegram channels published and then deleted footage of several strikes on UN vehicles, claiming operators from Russia's 18th Combined Arms Army carried out the attack and describing the clearly marked vehicle as a 'dual-use' target. UNHCR said it was the second incident affecting marked UN aid transport in a week and that 56 incidents affecting humanitarian workers had been recorded from January through April 2026, killing three and injuring ten.
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Marked UN aid convoy hit in Kherson, sharpening concerns over repeated attacks on humanitarian relief operations
