title: Zelensky says intelligence shows Russian plans to target Kyiv decision-making centers
detail: President Volodymyr Zelensky said Ukrainian military and intelligence briefings showed Russian plans to strike political and military 'decision-making centers' in Kyiv and other cities, including the President's Office on Bankova Street, the state residence in Koncha Zaspa, government buildings, military command posts, and underground shelters. Kyiv Independent reported that HUR-obtained documents included coordinates for roughly two dozen targets, while RBC-Ukraine quoted Zelensky saying Moscow had revived long-developed targeting plans after Iran and was actively trying to locate Ukrainian leadership. Zelensky said Ukraine had taken the information into account and argued Russia should negotiate a dignified peace rather than search for new ways to intimidate Ukraine.
tn: Bankova Street and other Kyiv decision-making centers
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Ukrainian leadership warns of documented Russian plans for strikes on political and military command sites

title: Russia launches 294-drone overnight barrage; at least three killed and 63 wounded across Ukraine
detail: Russia attacked Ukraine overnight on May 15-16 with 294 Shahed-type strike drones, including jet-powered variants, plus Gerbera, Italmas, and Parodiya decoy UAVs from Oryol, Kursk, Bryansk, Millerovo, Shatalovo, Primorsko-Akhtarsk, and occupied Crimea, Ukraine's Air Force said. Ukrainian defenses shot down or suppressed 269 drones, but 20 UAV hits were recorded at 15 locations and debris fell at nine more while several drones remained airborne in the morning. Kyiv Independent, citing regional authorities, reported at least three civilians killed and 63 injured over the past day: one killed and 23 injured in Kherson Oblast, one killed and 21 injured in Zaporizhzhia Oblast, one killed and four injured in Donetsk Oblast, plus injuries in Sumy, Kharkiv, Odesa, and Dnipropetrovsk oblasts.
tn: Nationwide Ukraine drone attack, with hits in multiple oblasts
dl: MAJOR — Continued mass drone campaign records impacts in 24 locations and civilian casualties across multiple Ukrainian regions

title: Russian drones damage Odesa port infrastructure and hit central Kharkiv transit facilities
detail: Regional officials said Russian drones struck southern Odesa Oblast overnight, damaging residential and port infrastructure: a five-story apartment building and an auto dealership on its ground floor caught fire or were damaged, a one-story home was hit, two people were injured, and a port warehouse and administrative building had windows shattered. In Kharkiv, a morning drone strike hit the central Shevchenkivskyi district, damaging two metro exits, the city's electric transport contact network, a trolleybus, and a public transport stop, injuring one civilian; earlier overnight strikes also hit Kholodnohirskyi and Osnovianskyi districts. The Independent reported the Odesa attack also affected power infrastructure, leaving nearly 40 settlements and more than 22,000 consumers without electricity.
tn: Odesa Oblast port/residential sites and central Kharkiv transit infrastructure
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Overnight drone wave damages port, residential, power, and public-transit infrastructure in Odesa and Kharkiv

title: Ukrainian drones reportedly strike Nevinnomyssk Azot chemical plant in southern Russia
detail: Militarnyi, citing Astra analysis of photos and videos, reported that Ukrainian strike drones hit the Nevinnomyssk Azot chemical plant in Stavropol Krai overnight on May 15-16 after a regional drone threat was declared, with footage showing a fire on the plant's grounds. Regional authorities claimed there was no ground damage, but Militarnyi noted the plant had been targeted repeatedly in earlier Ukrainian drone campaigns. Nevinnomyssk Azot, part of EuroChem, is Russia's largest producer of nitrogen fertilizers and ammonia and produces nitric and acetic acids; Reuters previously reported that Nevinnomyssk Azot and Novomoskovsky Azot supplied chemicals used by the Sverdlov explosives plant for artillery-shell components.
tn: Nevinnomyssk Azot chemical plant, Stavropol Krai
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Long-range Ukrainian drones reportedly hit a major Russian chemical plant linked to explosive precursor supply chains

title: Gerasimov repeats inflated Kupyansk-Borova-Lyman claims as ISW says Ukrainian lines are holding
detail: Russian Chief of the General Staff Valery Gerasimov met the Western Grouping of Forces on May 16 and claimed Russian troops were advancing west of Kupyansk toward Shevchenkove, had entered Velyka Shapkivka, seized Borova and Kutkivka, and held large portions of Shyikivka and Lyman. ISW assessed those claims as highly inaccurate, saying Russian forces do not hold consolidated positions in Kupyansk, Velyka Shapkivka, Shyikivka, Kutkivka or Lyman and have only infiltrated small percentages of Kupyansk, Kutkivka and Lyman; ISW also said Ukrainian defensive lines are holding against Russia's spring-summer offensive and Ukrainian forces are contesting the tactical initiative in several sectors. The assessment warned that such 'beautiful reports' may be shaping Russian operational planning around objectives, including Shevchenkove, that Russian forces cannot yet realistically pursue.
tn: Kupyansk-Borova-Lyman axis, Kharkiv and Donetsk oblasts
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Senior Russian command's contested battlefield claims highlight information warfare and potential planning distortions on the Kupyansk-Borova-Lyman axis

sources.name: Institute for the Study of War

title: US lets waiver for Russian seaborne oil purchases lapse after temporary Hormuz-related extension
detail: The Trump administration allowed a sanctions waiver to lapse on May 16 that had temporarily let countries including India buy Russian seaborne oil after a month-long extension intended to ease supply shortages and high prices linked to Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz. Reuters reported that Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent had previously said he would not renew the general license for purchases of Russian oil stored on tankers, and The Guardian noted that no renewal notice had been posted by early Saturday afternoon in Washington. Senators Jeanne Shaheen and Elizabeth Warren had urged the administration not to renew the waiver, arguing it was sending revenue to Russia's war effort without clear evidence of lowering U.S. fuel costs.
tn: Washington, D.C.; Russian seaborne oil sanctions policy
dl: SIGNIFICANT — U.S. sanctions relief window for Russian seaborne oil expires, potentially tightening pressure on Moscow's wartime energy revenues

title: Russia attacks Ukraine with 287 drones, hitting Dnipro, Kharkiv and other locations
detail: Russia launched 287 Shahed-type strike drones, including jet-powered versions, Gerbera, Italmas and Parodiya decoys, from Oryol, Kursk, Bryansk, Millerovo, Shatalovo and Primorsko-Akhtarsk overnight on May 16-17, Ukraine's Air Force said. Ukrainian defenses shot down or electronically suppressed 279 drones, while eight strike UAVs hit seven locations and debris fell in seven more, with additional drone groups still airborne in the morning. RBC-Ukraine reported impacts in Dnipro, where an industrial facility and private homes were damaged and two men sought medical help, and in Kharkiv, where drones hit or fell in Saltivskyi, Kyivskyi and Kholodnohirskyi districts, setting seven cars on fire, damaging about ten more vehicles, breaking apartment-block windows and damaging street lighting infrastructure.
tn: Nationwide Ukraine drone attack, with reported impacts in Dnipro and Kharkiv
dl: MAJOR — Another nearly 300-drone Russian barrage causes impacts across Ukraine and damages urban infrastructure in Dnipro and Kharkiv

title: More than 500 Ukrainian drones hit Russia in one of the war's largest barrages, killing four
detail: Ukraine launched a mass overnight drone attack on Russia on May 16-17, with Russia's Defense Ministry claiming air defenses shot down 556 drones across 14 regions, occupied Crimea, and the Black and Azov seas. The Moscow region was among the hardest hit: Governor Andrey Vorobyov said a woman was killed when a UAV struck a private house and two men were killed in Pogorelki, with four people wounded and infrastructure facilities targeted; Moscow authorities reported more than 80 drones intercepted over the capital, 12 people wounded, minor debris damage, three residential buildings damaged, and construction workers wounded near an oil and gas refinery whose production was not disrupted. Ukrainska Pravda reported numerous explosions around Moscow and Moscow Oblast, a fire at the Elma technology park, a possible attempt against a Moscow oil refinery, and flight restrictions at Vnukovo, Domodedovo, Ramenskoye/Zhukovsky and Sheremetyevo airports, while Belgorod authorities reported one additional death in a drone attack on a lorry in Shebekino district.
tn: Moscow region, Moscow, Belgorod region and other Russian regions
dl: MAJOR — One of Ukraine's largest long-range drone barrages of the war hits Moscow-area infrastructure, disrupts airports and causes reported civilian casualties in Russia

title: Russian drones, missiles and shelling hit Odesa, Dnipro, Zaporizhzhia and Kherson, killing one and wounding over 30
detail: Reuters reported that Russia launched drones, airstrikes and shelling overnight on May 17-18, targeting Odesa, Dnipro, Zaporizhzhia and Kherson. In Odesa, drones hit residential buildings, a school and a kindergarten, injuring an 11-year-old boy and a 59-year-old man. Dnipro was struck by missiles, injuring 18 people including two children; Zaporizhzhia reported three injuries, and Kherson officials said one person was killed and nine wounded. The Kyiv Independent separately reported at least 20 injuries in Dnipro and Odesa and damage to a 24-storey residential building, an educational lecture hall and a kindergarten.
tn: Dnipro, Odesa, Zaporizhzhia and Kherson, Ukraine
dl: MAJOR — Overnight Russian missile, drone, airstrike and shelling attacks kill one and wound more than 30 across southern and southeastern Ukraine

title: Russian drone strikes Chinese-owned KSL DEYANG cargo vessel en route to Ukraine's Pivdennyi port
detail: A Russian drone struck the Chinese-owned cargo vessel KSL DEYANG, sailing under a Marshall Islands flag, off Ukraine's coast early on May 18 while it was headed to Pivdennyi port in Odesa Oblast to load iron ore concentrate. The ship was empty at the time, sustained only minor damage, and the crew extinguished the resulting fire with no reported casualties. Ukrainian navy spokesperson Dmytro Pletenchuk questioned whether the strike was a serious mistake, noting the vessel's Chinese ownership as Russia prepared for high-level engagement with Beijing.
tn: Black Sea approaches to Pivdennyi port, Odesa Oblast
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Russian drone attack damages a Chinese-owned merchant vessel bound for a Ukrainian Black Sea port

title: Estonian intelligence chief says sanctions and manpower losses leave Putin with 'very difficult choices' in Ukraine
detail: Reuters reported that Estonia's foreign intelligence chief Kaupo Rosin assessed Russia is losing more personnel in Ukraine than it is recruiting, while financial and oil sanctions are straining Moscow's resources and Russia's economy contracted by 0.3% in the first quarter. Rosin said Russian forces have been making some of their slowest advances since 2023 and that a general mobilization would be deeply unpopular, but another European intelligence chief said there was still no sign Moscow was softening its objectives, including demands over the Donbas. Rosin urged allies to keep increasing sanctions pressure and warned Russia would likely retain large forces near Ukraine and seek expanded military dominance along NATO's border after the war.
tn: Tallinn, Estonia; NATO eastern flank assessment of Russia's war effort
dl: SIGNIFICANT — NATO-frontline intelligence assessment says sanctions and attrition are narrowing Russia's options, though Moscow shows no peace breakthrough signal

title: Russia launches 209 drones across Ukraine, killing six and hitting Naftogaz gas infrastructure for third straight day
detail: Russian forces attacked Ukraine overnight on May 18-19 with 209 Shahed-type, Gerbera and Italmas loitering munitions and Parodiya decoys, Ukraine's Air Force said, with air defences destroying or suppressing 180 drones. Hits by 27 UAVs were recorded at 15 locations, while regional authorities reported at least six civilians killed and at least 34 injured over the preceding day. The strikes included multiple Naftogaz gas infrastructure facilities in Chernihiv Oblast, where the company said critically important production equipment was damaged and personnel were evacuated, continuing a third consecutive day of Russian attacks on the state oil and gas operator's facilities. Kherson, Kharkiv, Donetsk, Dnipropetrovsk, Sumy, Zaporizhzhia and Chernihiv oblasts also reported heavy shelling, drone strikes or civilian damage.
tn: Nationwide Ukraine attack, including Chernihiv gas infrastructure and multiple frontline oblasts
dl: MAJOR — Russia fires 209 drones across Ukraine, causing dozens of casualties and damaging Naftogaz gas production infrastructure for a third straight day

title: Russian drone strike damages Izmail Danube port infrastructure, Ukraine's largest Danube export hub
detail: A Russian drone attack in the early hours of May 19 damaged port infrastructure in Izmail, Odesa Oblast, Ukraine's largest port on the Danube River and a key grain-export and logistics hub. Local officials said nearly all aerial attack weapons were destroyed and no casualties or major destruction were reported, but Reuters images and official posts showed firefighters battling a blaze at a building with blown-out windows. Al Jazeera reported the attack lasted roughly from 1 a.m. to 3 a.m. local time and followed another Russian strike on Izmail port infrastructure earlier in May.
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Russian drones again damage infrastructure at Izmail, Ukraine's largest Danube port and an important wartime export route

title: Ukrainian drones hit Yaroslavl industrial facility and force airport restrictions deep inside Russia
detail: Russian regional authorities said Ukrainian UAVs attacked Yaroslavl Oblast on May 19, with most drones shot down but one hitting an industrial facility and causing a fire; no casualties were reported. Governor Mikhail Yevrayev temporarily closed traffic leaving Yaroslavl toward Moscow, and Rosaviatsiya reported flight restrictions at Yaroslavl and several other Russian airports. Ukrainska Pravda noted that Yaroslavl hosts the Yaroslavl oil pumping station and Slavneft-YANOS refinery, one of Russia's largest refineries at roughly 15 million tonnes of annual capacity, which had already been targeted earlier in May. Reuters also reported four Ukraine-launched drones downed en route to Moscow and a separate Ukrainian attack in Kursk Oblast that killed one woman and wounded two people.
tn: Yaroslavl, Russia; wider Moscow and Kursk-region drone activity
dl: MAJOR — Ukrainian drones reach Yaroslavl's industrial/refining area northeast of Moscow, disrupting roads and airports as long-range strikes continue

title: Reuters analysis says Ukraine's expanding mid-range drone campaign is disrupting Russian logistics, air defences and oil infrastructure
detail: Reuters reported that Ukraine has scaled up systematic mid-range drone strikes, typically 30-180 kilometres behind the front, against Russian radars, short- and medium-range air defences, communications, logistics and large military vehicles. Ukraine's Unmanned Systems Forces commander Robert Brovdi said the role of such 'middle strikes' is currently decisive and claimed his forces have destroyed at least 129 air-defence systems this year in Russian-occupied areas, a figure Reuters could not independently verify. Analysts told Reuters the attacks cannot by themselves turn the tide but are helping slow Russia's battlefield advances, stretch logistics, force air defences away from the front and open corridors for long-range strikes on refineries and military facilities; Zelenskyy said middle strikes had doubled compared with March and quadrupled since February.
tn: Ukraine and Russian-occupied territories; Russian rear-area logistics and air-defence network
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Reuters assessment highlights Ukraine's rapidly scaling mid-range drone campaign as a systematic operational tool against Russian logistics and air defences

title: Trump signals conditional support for tougher Russia sanctions while US extends Russian-oil sanctions waiver
detail: A senior White House official told Reuters that President Donald Trump would consider signing a sanctions bill targeting countries doing business with Russia, including buyers of Russian oil and gas, if the package includes a carve-out preserving the president's ultimate decision-making authority. The Graham-Fitzpatrick legislation had been delayed over Trump's concerns, while the White House said negotiations with Moscow to end the war are continuing. Separately, Reuters reported the US extended for 30 days a sanctions waiver on Russian oil transported by sea after requests from several countries and concerns over oil shortages and higher global prices; Ukraine's sanctions policy commissioner Vladyslav Vlasiuk estimated Russia may have gained more than $5 billion in extra revenue during two months of sanctions easing.
tn: Washington, DC; US sanctions policy toward Russia
dl: SIGNIFICANT — US policy signals remain mixed as Trump backs tougher Russia sanctions only with presidential discretion while Washington extends an oil-sanctions waiver

title: NATO jet shoots down Ukrainian drone over Estonia as Kyiv blames Russian electronic warfare
detail: Reuters reported that a Ukrainian drone entered Estonian airspace from Russia around noon on May 19 and was shot down at 12:14 p.m. local time by a Romanian NATO fighter jet with a single missile. Ukraine's Foreign Ministry apologized to Estonia and said Russian electronic warfare had redirected the drone, while Estonia's military cited heavy GPS spoofing and jamming by Russia. Latvia also issued air-threat alerts over possible drone incursions near its Russian border, temporarily suspending trains and school exams before later saying it had not confirmed an incursion. NATO confirmed the shootdown and said its Baltic air-policing forces were ready to respond to air threats.
tn: Southeastern Estonia, near the Russia border
dl: SIGNIFICANT — NATO aircraft shoot down a Ukraine-launched drone over Estonia after Kyiv and Tallinn say Russian electronic warfare diverted it into alliance airspace

title: Russia launches 154 drones and ballistic missile across Ukraine, killing eight and injuring at least 52
detail: Russia attacked multiple Ukrainian regions overnight into May 20 with 154 drones and one ballistic missile, Ukraine's Air Force said, with 131 drones shot down and direct hits by a missile and 23 drones recorded at 20 locations. The Kyiv Independent, citing regional officials, reported eight people killed and at least 52 injured nationwide. In Konotop, Sumy Oblast, a strike killed a woman, injured at least 11 people and collapsed three stories of an apartment building; four more people were killed elsewhere in Sumy Oblast. In Dnipro, two people were killed and six injured as a food storage warehouse, houses and vehicles were damaged. Kharkiv, Donetsk, Zaporizhzhia, Chernihiv and Odesa oblasts also reported casualties or civilian damage, including seven wounded in Zaporizhzhia Oblast, three of them children.
tn: Konotop, Sumy Oblast; Dnipro and multiple Ukrainian regions
dl: SEVERE — Nationwide Russian overnight attack kills eight, wounds at least 52, and partially collapses a Konotop apartment block while damaging civilian sites across Ukraine

title: Ukrainian drones target Kstovo oil refinery and Nevinnomyssk Azot chemical plant for second time in a week
detail: Ukrainian drones reportedly struck or targeted the Lukoil-Nizhegorodnefteorgsintez oil refinery in Kstovo, Nizhny Novgorod Oblast, and the Nevinnomyssk Azot chemical plant in Stavropol Krai overnight on May 20. The Kyiv Independent cited Russian Telegram channels reporting drones, explosions and smoke near the Kstovo refinery, one of Russia's largest oil refining enterprises with roughly 17 million tons of annual processing capacity and production of gasoline, diesel and aviation fuel used by Russian forces. Russian channels also reported explosions and a fire near Nevinnomyssk Azot, a major producer of ammonia and ammonium nitrate used in explosives and artillery-shell production; local and regional officials claimed air defenses repelled attacks on industrial zones. Reuters separately reported Russia's Defence Ministry said 273 drones were downed overnight across Russia, with drone attacks around Nevinnomyssk, Kstovo, Leningrad Oblast, Moscow, Tula and Belgorod.
tn: Nevinnomyssk, Stavropol Krai; Kstovo, Nizhny Novgorod Oblast, Russia
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Ukraine's deep-strike campaign again targets major Russian fuel and chemical facilities, while Moscow reports 273 drones downed overnight

title: Syrskyi says Ukrainian assaults have outnumbered Russian attacks for first time as Russia masses 99,000 troops near Pokrovsk
detail: In an interview published on May 20, Ukrainian Commander-in-Chief Oleksandr Syrskyi said Ukrainian forces had intensified counterattacks and that, for the first time, the number of Ukrainian offensive operations on one day exceeded the number of Russian offensive operations, though he warned Russia remained strong. Syrskyi said Russia had concentrated about 99,000 personnel in the Pokrovsk direction, the largest Russian grouping on the front, and was also planning possible northern operations from Belarus. Ukrinform, citing Ukraine's General Staff as of 22:00 on May 19, reported 189 combat clashes across the front and 40 Russian attacks in the Pokrovsk sector, while also noting Ukrainian forces had advanced up to three kilometers into Russian defensive lines near Pokrovsk.
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Ukraine reports a rare shift toward more active counterattacks while Russia concentrates its largest frontline grouping, about 99,000 troops, near Pokrovsk

title: ISW says Ukraine is regaining tactical initiative in several sectors while Russia advances in Sumy and Pokrovsk directions
detail: The Institute for the Study of War's May 20 assessment said Ukrainian forces appear to be regaining the tactical initiative in several parts of the front, with reported Ukrainian advances in the Kupyansk direction, the Kostyantynivka-Druzhkivka tactical area and western Zaporizhia Oblast. ISW also assessed that Russian forces advanced in the Sumy and Pokrovsk directions, underscoring that initiative remains contested. Kyiv Post's summary of the assessment highlighted the same mixed territorial picture and noted Russia's May 20 launch of one ballistic missile, 154 drones overnight and an additional 84 drones during the day.
tn: Frontline Ukraine: Kupyansk, Kostyantynivka-Druzhkivka, western Zaporizhia, Sumy and Pokrovsk sectors
dl: SIGNIFICANT — ISW assesses Ukrainian tactical gains in multiple sectors alongside Russian advances in Sumy and Pokrovsk, showing a fluid and contested front

sources.name: Institute for the Study of War

title: Zelensky says Russia is planning possible Chernihiv-Kyiv offensive scenarios involving Belarus
detail: President Volodymyr Zelensky warned on May 20 that Ukrainian intelligence had analyzed Russian planning for possible offensive operations from the Belarus-Bryansk direction toward Chernihiv and Kyiv, potentially with deeper Belarusian involvement. Zelensky said Ukraine had issued orders to strengthen defenses in the northern sector, was preparing preventive steps regarding Belarus and relevant areas of Russia, and would intensify diplomatic pressure on Minsk. The Guardian reported Zelensky said Kyiv knew of five Russian scenarios and would increase forces in the sector, while Ukraine's border guards said they had not detected movement of equipment or personnel directly at the Belarus border but saw Russian pressure on Belarus.
tn: Chernihiv-Kyiv direction and Belarus-Bryansk border sector
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Kyiv orders northern defenses reinforced after intelligence on possible Russian offensive scenarios from the Belarus-Bryansk direction toward Chernihiv and Kyiv

title: Russia launches Iskander missile and 116 drones as strikes kill five and injure 41 across Ukraine
detail: Russian attacks over the previous day killed at least five people and injured 41 across Ukraine, the Kyiv Independent reported, citing local authorities. Ukraine's Air Force said Russia launched one Iskander-M ballistic missile and 116 drones overnight, with 109 drones intercepted; the missile and at least five drones struck five locations, with falling debris recorded at four more sites. In Chernihiv Oblast, a Geran long-range drone hit a farm bus in the Semenivka community, killing one agricultural worker and injuring two others, while additional drone strikes hit a farm warehouse and wounded a tractor driver. Donetsk, Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk, Sumy, Kherson and Zaporizhzhia oblasts also reported deaths, injuries, shelling, guided-bomb strikes or drone attacks.
tn: Nationwide Ukraine attacks, including Chernihiv, Donetsk, Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk, Sumy, Kherson and Zaporizhzhia oblasts
dl: SEVERE — Russian overnight missile-and-drone attack and regional strikes kill five, wound 41, and hit agricultural workers and civilian infrastructure across Ukraine

title: Ukrainian drones reportedly strike Rosneft's Syzran oil refinery deep inside Russia, killing two
detail: A Ukrainian drone attack overnight on May 21 reportedly struck an oil refining unit at Rosneft's Syzran Oil Refinery in Samara Oblast, about 700 kilometers northeast of Ukraine's border with Russia, according to local residents cited by the Kyiv Independent. Samara Oblast Governor Vyacheslav Fedorishchev said two people were killed in the drone attack but did not confirm whether infrastructure was damaged. Reuters also reported the two deaths and noted the large refinery in Syzran, while authorities said three more people were injured by drones around Shebekino in Belgorod Oblast. Kyiv says Russian energy facilities are legitimate military targets because they fuel and finance Moscow's war effort.
dl: MAJOR — Ukrainian long-range drones reportedly hit a Rosneft refinery unit in Syzran, roughly 700 km from Ukraine, with local authorities reporting two deaths

title: Reuters: Ukrainian drone campaign forces central Russia's major refineries to halt or scale back output
detail: Reuters reported May 20, and the Kyiv Independent summarized May 21, that all of central Russia's major oil refineries have fully halted or reduced fuel production after recent Ukrainian drone strikes. The affected refineries include Kirishi, Nizhny Novgorod/Kstovo, Ryazan, Yaroslavl and Moscow, with combined capacity of about 238,000 tons per day or 83 million tons per year — roughly a quarter of Russia's total refining capacity. The sites account for more than 30% of Russian gasoline output and about 25% of diesel output. The disruption has coincided with Moscow's gasoline export ban and adds pressure to Russia's federal budget, where oil and gas revenue remains central.
tn: Central Russia refinery network, including Moscow, Ryazan, Yaroslavl, Nizhny Novgorod/Kstovo and Kirishi
dl: CRITICAL — Ukrainian drone strikes force shutdowns or reduced output across refineries representing about a quarter of Russia's refining capacity

title: Russian daytime drone strike hits apartment blocks in Dnipro, injuring 19
detail: A Russian daytime drone attack struck Dnipro on May 21, hitting two multi-story apartment buildings and sparking a fire as drones were reported over the city. Local officials said at least 19 people were injured, including three children; a 13-year-old boy and seven adults were hospitalized. Dnipro Mayor Borys Filatov said most casualties suffered shrapnel wounds, burns or lacerations, and that eight residential buildings, coffee shops and dozens of vehicles were damaged, with the roof of a four-story building collapsing above two stairwells. Regional officials also reported nearly 30 Russian drone and artillery attacks across four Dnipropetrovsk Oblast districts that day.
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Daytime Russian drone strike damages Dnipro apartment blocks and injures 19 civilians, including three children

title: Russia and Belarus complete combined nuclear drills as Moscow deepens military control over Belarus
detail: The Institute for the Study of War reported that Russia and Belarus completed the second stage of combined nuclear-force exercises on May 21, with Vladimir Putin and Alexander Lukashenko presiding. Russia's Defense Ministry said Russian forces delivered nuclear munitions to Belarus while Belarusian forces practiced receiving, equipping and moving specialized munitions and Iskander-M launch vehicles. Moscow also claimed launches of Yars and Sineva intercontinental ballistic missiles, a Zircon hypersonic missile, cruise missiles from Tu-95MS bombers, a Kinzhal missile and an Iskander-M missile. ISW assessed the drills underscore Russia's ability to use Belarus for future operations and its expanding de facto control over Belarus.
tn: Belarus and Russian strategic test ranges
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Russia-Belarus nuclear drills rehearse deployment and handling of nuclear-capable systems, highlighting Belarus as a potential Russian military springboard

sources.name: Institute for the Study of War

title: Russia attacks Ukraine with 124 drones overnight; Ukraine says 115 neutralized
detail: Ukraine's Air Force said Russia launched 124 Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas and other attack or decoy drones at Ukraine overnight on May 22. Ukrainian air defenses, electronic-warfare units, mobile fire groups and UAV teams shot down or neutralized 115 drones, according to preliminary data. RBC-Ukraine reported seven Russian drone strikes at five locations, with debris from downed drones found in five more areas, and said the attack was still ongoing at the time of the report with several drones remaining in Ukrainian airspace.
tn: Nationwide Ukraine airspace and strike locations
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Russia launches another large overnight drone wave, with 124 drones sent and seven strike impacts reported despite 115 neutralized

title: Ukrainian General Staff reports 253 combat clashes, with Pokrovsk sector again the fiercest front
detail: Ukraine's General Staff said as of 8:00 a.m. on May 22 that 253 combat engagements occurred across the front over the previous 24 hours, with the highest intensity in the Pokrovsk sector, where Ukrainian forces repelled 52 Russian assaults near settlements including Zatyshok, Nikanorivka, Rodynske, Hryshyne, Kotlyne, Udachne, Bilytske and Myrne. Russian forces also conducted one missile strike, 95 air strikes, dropped 288 guided aerial bombs, used 9,655 kamikaze drones and carried out 3,285 shelling attacks. Ukrainian aviation, missile forces and artillery struck 12 concentrations of Russian personnel and three UAV command posts.
tn: Pokrovsk sector and wider frontline, Ukraine
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Frontline tempo remains extremely high with 253 clashes in a day and 52 Russian assaults repelled in the Pokrovsk sector alone

title: Germany urges EU to consider associate membership for Ukraine to support talks and integration
detail: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz urged EU leaders to consider offering Ukraine an 'associate membership' arrangement and to use it to revive talks aimed at ending the war, according to a letter reported by AP and summarized by The Guardian. Under the proposal, Ukraine would participate in EU meetings without voting rights and could have non-voting associate representatives in the European Commission and European Parliament, even though such a category does not currently exist under EU rules. Merz argued the proposal could support a negotiated peace solution and European security, while Zelensky welcomed signs of possible progress in Ukraine's EU path.
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Germany floats an interim EU associate-membership track for Ukraine, linking integration incentives to peace diplomacy and European security

title: Ukraine strikes Yaroslavl oil refinery for fourth time in May, Zelensky confirms
detail: President Volodymyr Zelensky confirmed on May 22 that Ukraine had carried out long-range drone strikes against Russian oil infrastructure, including the Yaroslavl oil refinery about 700 kilometers from Ukraine's border. The Kyiv Independent reported that the Yaroslavl attack was the fourth strike on the refinery in May, after earlier hits on May 8, 13 and 19. Social-media footage and Astra geolocation suggested the target was the Slavneft-YANOS refinery, one of Russia's five largest refineries, with annual processing capacity of about 15 million tons. Russian authorities claimed air defenses repelled the attack and reported no casualties or damage, while Ukraine framed the strikes as 'long-range sanctions' against facilities fueling Russia's war effort.
dl: MAJOR — Ukraine confirms another long-range strike on the Slavneft-YANOS/Yaroslavl refinery, its fourth reported hit there in May and part of the campaign against Russian fuel infrastructure

title: Ukraine says it hit Rubikon drone headquarters near Starobilsk as Russia claims dormitory casualties
detail: Ukraine's General Staff rejected Russian claims that a Ukrainian drone strike killed civilians at a dormitory in occupied Luhansk Oblast, saying the May 22 overnight operation struck Russian military targets including an oil refinery, ammunition depots, air-defense systems, command posts and a headquarters of the Rubikon Center for Unmanned Technologies in the Starobilsk area. Russian officials claimed six people were killed and dozens injured, including children, and Vladimir Putin ordered retaliation options while Moscow called a UN Security Council meeting. Ukraine called the Russian account misleading information and said the Rubikon unit conducts drone attacks against Ukrainian civilians and civilian infrastructure.
tn: Starobilsk area, occupied Luhansk Oblast, Ukraine
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Ukraine reports strikes on Russian military infrastructure and a Rubikon drone-unit headquarters near Starobilsk, while Russia claims civilian dormitory casualties and vows retaliation

title: Russian attacks kill five and injure 62 across Ukraine as 124-drone wave hits nine locations
detail: Russian attacks across Ukraine killed at least five people and injured at least 62 over the previous day, the Kyiv Independent reported May 23, citing local authorities. Ukraine's Air Force said Russia launched 124 strike and decoy drones overnight from multiple directions, including Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas and Parodiya-type drones; air defenses shot down or suppressed 102, while 12 strike drones hit nine locations and debris fell in five more. Kherson Oblast reported one killed and 20 injured as homes, a church and vehicles were damaged, while Kharkiv Oblast reported three killed and five injured, Zaporizhzhia one killed and two injured, Dnipropetrovsk 16 injured, Sumy 12 injured and Donetsk seven injured.
tn: Nationwide Ukraine attacks, including Kherson, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, Dnipropetrovsk, Sumy and Donetsk oblasts
dl: SEVERE — Russia launches another 124-drone wave and regional attacks kill five civilians and wound 62, with Kherson, Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia among the hardest-hit oblasts

title: Ukrainian drone attack sparks fire at Novorossiysk oil depot, Russia's key Black Sea export hub
detail: A Ukrainian drone attack caused a fire at an oil depot in Russia's Black Sea port of Novorossiysk, the city's mayor said early May 23. Officials said falling UAV debris set fire to the oil depot, with several technical and administrative buildings burning and drone fragments falling on the fuel terminal site; two people were wounded. The Moscow Times noted that Novorossiysk handles around one fifth of Russia's crude oil shipments and is the country's largest Black Sea export hub. Russia's Defense Ministry claimed air defenses intercepted 348 Ukrainian drones overnight, while Ukraine has continued long-range strikes aimed at reducing Russian oil revenue and military fuel supplies.
dl: MAJOR — Ukrainian drones ignite an oil-depot fire at Novorossiysk, Russia's largest Black Sea export hub, wounding two and threatening a key crude-shipment terminal

title: Ukrainian General Staff reports 216 frontline clashes, with Pokrovsk and Huliaipole sectors hottest
detail: Ukraine's General Staff reported 216 combat clashes over the previous day as of 8:00 a.m. on May 23, with the fiercest fighting in the Pokrovsk and Huliaipole sectors. Ukrinform reported that Russian forces carried out 83 airstrikes, dropped 264 guided aerial bombs, launched 9,753 kamikaze drones and conducted 3,195 attacks on Ukrainian troop positions and populated areas. Ukrainian defenders stopped 42 Russian assaults in the Pokrovsk sector near settlements including Rodynske, Pokrovsk, Hryshyne and Udachne, and 32 attacks in the Huliaipole sector near Nove Zaporizhzhia, Zlahoda, Solodke, Staroukrainka and other settlements. Ukrainian aviation, missile forces and artillery struck a Russian command post, nine troop concentration areas and an artillery system.
tn: Pokrovsk and Huliaipole sectors, eastern and southeastern Ukraine
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Frontline tempo remains intense with 216 clashes in a day, including 42 Russian assaults near Pokrovsk and 32 in the Huliaipole sector

title: Russian missile strike injures nine in Odesa Oblast as drone attacks wound nine in Balakliia
detail: Russian attacks hit southern and eastern Ukraine on the evening of May 23 as officials warned of a broader overnight strike. Odesa Oblast governor Oleh Kiper said a Russian missile struck civilian infrastructure, injuring nine people, including three children aged 8 to 12; seven of the wounded were hospitalized and one adult was in serious condition. In Kharkiv Oblast, local authorities said Russian drone attacks on Balakliia injured nine people, while Kyiv officials reported explosions as Russian drones approached the capital before the larger May 24 missile-and-drone barrage.
tn: Odesa Oblast and Balakliia, Kharkiv Oblast, Ukraine
dl: MODERATE — Russian missile and drone strikes wound 18 civilians across Odesa and Kharkiv oblasts, including three children, hours before the larger Kyiv-focused barrage

title: Russia launches 690-weapon missile and drone barrage centered on Kyiv, killing two and injuring over 80
detail: Russia carried out one of its largest combined missile and drone attacks of the year overnight on May 24, with Kyiv and the surrounding region the main target. Ukraine's Air Force said Russia launched 90 missiles and 600 drones, including an Oreshnik medium-range ballistic missile that struck the Bila Tserkva district, two Kinzhals, three Zircons, Iskander/S-400 ballistic missiles, cruise missiles and 600 Shahed-type and decoy drones; Ukrainian defenses reported shooting down or jamming 55 missiles and 549 drones. Kyiv officials and President Volodymyr Zelensky reported two people killed and more than 80 injured, with damage in every district of the capital including residential towers, schools, a water-supply facility, a market, warehouses, the Ukrposhta headquarters, cultural institutions and metro-area infrastructure. Poland scrambled Polish and allied aircraft during the attack, and the strike followed warnings from Zelensky and the U.S. Embassy that Russia was preparing a major air attack that could involve Oreshnik.
tn: Kyiv and Bila Tserkva, Kyiv Oblast, Ukraine
dl: CRITICAL — Russia fires 90 missiles and 600 drones in a Kyiv-centered saturation attack, uses Oreshnik against Bila Tserkva, kills two and injures over 80 while damaging residential, civic and cultural sites across the capital

title: Russian drone and artillery strikes wound 23 civilians across Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk, Kherson and Zaporizhzhia oblasts
detail: Russian forces continued tactical attacks into the morning of May 24 across frontline and border regions, wounding 23 civilians, the Kyiv Post reported from regional military administrations. In Bohodukhiv, Kharkiv Oblast, a Russian strike drone hit a residential area and wounded 12 people while damaging an apartment block and 15 vehicles. In Nikopol and Marhanets in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast, artillery and kamikaze drones wounded seven people, including a three-month-old infant and a four-year-old child, with two men in critical condition. Russian shelling critically wounded a woman in Kherson's Korabelnyi district, and a loitering munition wounded two 63-year-old residents near homes in Bilenke, Zaporizhzhia Oblast.
tn: Bohodukhiv, Nikopol, Kherson and Bilenke, Ukraine
dl: MODERATE — Russian drone and artillery strikes wound 23 civilians across four regions, including an infant and a child in Nikopol and 12 people in Bohodukhiv

title: Ukraine sanctions Russian missile commanders and shadow military logistics vessels after major Kyiv attack warnings
detail: President Volodymyr Zelensky signed decrees imposing new sanctions on 127 Russian personnel involved in missile strikes on Ukraine and on 29 civilian merchant vessels used for Russian military logistics. Ukraine's Presidential Office said the first package targets long-range aviation, missile and artillery commanders linked to more than 4,100 air-launched cruise missile launches and over 1,100 Iskander missile attacks, including strikes on civilian and critical infrastructure such as Okhmatdyt Children's Hospital, Groza, Sumy and Chernihiv. The second package targets ships carrying weapons, ammunition, military equipment and personnel for Russia's Defense Ministry, with Kyiv saying it will seek partner synchronization for vessels not yet under U.S., EU or U.K. restrictions.
dl: LIGHT — Ukraine expands sanctions against Russian missile commanders and military logistics vessels, aiming to raise accountability and disrupt arms supply channels

title: Russia launches 262-drone follow-on attack across Ukraine after massive Kyiv barrage; air defenses neutralize 246
detail: Russia followed its May 24 missile-and-drone barrage with another large overnight UAV attack beginning around 17:30 on May 24 and continuing into May 25. Ukraine's Air Force said Russian forces launched 262 strike and decoy drones, including Shahed-type UAVs, Gerbera, Italmas and Parodiya drones, from Oryol, Kursk, Shatalovo, Bryansk, Millerovo and Primorsko-Akhtarsk in Russia and Hvardiiske in occupied Crimea. Ukrainian aviation, air-defense missile units, electronic warfare teams, unmanned-systems forces and mobile fire groups shot down or suppressed 246 drones by 08:00 on May 25 across northern, southern and eastern Ukraine; 10 drones struck nine locations and debris fell in seven locations, with several drones still airborne when the report was issued.
dl: MODERATE — Russia sends 262 drones across Ukraine hours after the Kyiv-centered barrage; Ukraine reports neutralizing 246, though 10 drones hit nine locations

title: Russian strikes hit 49 settlements in Sumy Oblast, killing one civilian and injuring four
detail: Russian forces attacked 49 settlements across Sumy, Shostka, Okhtyrka, Konotop and Romny districts over the past day, using guided aerial bombs, attack and FPV drones and artillery, Sumy regional police reported. A 24-year-old man was killed in the Krasnopillia community when a Russian drone hit a vehicle; four other civilians were wounded in drone attacks or drone-dropped explosives in the Hlukhiv, Mykolaivka, Seredyna-Buda and Vorozhba communities. Police said apartment buildings, private homes, outbuildings, warehouses, vehicles and infrastructure facilities were damaged and opened war-crimes proceedings.
dl: MODERATE — Russian drones, bombs and artillery hit 49 Sumy Oblast settlements, killing one civilian in Krasnopillia and injuring four others

title: Missile and drone attack damages energy infrastructure in Russia's Belgorod region, killing one and disrupting power and water
detail: A missile and drone attack hit Russia's Belgorod region on May 25, damaging energy infrastructure and cutting power and water supplies, local authorities said according to Reuters. The strike also hit the city of Belgorod and left one man dead and another injured. Reuters reported the Belgorod attack alongside continuing strike reports on both sides of the border, including casualties from Russian attacks in Kherson and Zaporizhzhia and drone injuries in Russian-controlled Horlivka, while noting it could not independently verify battlefield claims.
dl: SIGNIFICANT — A missile and drone attack damages Belgorod-region energy infrastructure, cutting power and water and killing one person, Russian authorities say

title: General Staff reports 233 frontline clashes, with heaviest Russian pressure on Pokrovsk and Huliaipole axes
detail: Ukraine's General Staff reported 233 combat engagements over the previous day, with the heaviest fighting on the Pokrovsk and Huliaipole axes and no new confirmed Russian territorial captures in the report. Russian forces launched one missile strike with 89 missiles, 89 airstrikes with 261 guided aerial bombs, 9,975 kamikaze drones and 2,898 shelling attacks, including 58 MLRS attacks. Ukrainian defenders reported repelling 38 assault attempts on the Pokrovsk axis near settlements including Toretske, Novooleksandrivka, Rodynske, Hryshyne, Udachne, Molodetske, Novopavlivka and Muravka, while Russia carried out 28 assaults on the Huliaipole axis near Pryluky, Zaliznychne, Olenokostiantynivka and toward several nearby settlements.
tn: Pokrovsk and Huliaipole axes, eastern and southern Ukraine
dl: MODERATE — Ukraine reports 233 frontline clashes in one day, with Russian pressure concentrated on Pokrovsk and Huliaipole but no confirmed new capture in the update

title: Ukraine strikes Belets oil depot in Russia's Bryansk region and hits occupation command and ammunition sites
detail: Ukraine's General Staff said Ukrainian forces struck the Belets oil depot in Unecha, Bryansk Oblast, overnight into May 25, describing the facility as part of the Russian army's fuel supply chain, with damage still being assessed. Ukrainian reports said the wider strike set also hit ammunition depots and command nodes in Russian-occupied Crimea, Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia regions, as Kyiv continued targeting logistics and fuel infrastructure supporting Russia's invasion.
tn: Belets oil depot, Unecha, Bryansk Oblast, Russia
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Ukrainian deep strike targets Russian fuel logistics and occupation command infrastructure

title: Lavrov tells Rubio Russia will conduct systematic strikes on Kyiv and urges foreign evacuations
detail: Russian Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov told U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio that Russia would carry out systematic strikes on sites in Kyiv it alleged were connected to Ukraine's military, and Moscow urged foreign governments to evacuate diplomatic staff and citizens from the capital. Ukraine's foreign minister Andrii Sybiha called the warning Russian blackmail, while EU ambassador Katarina Mathernova said the EU mission would remain in Kyiv. Rubio later said Russia sent the warning to all embassies, not only the United States.
dl: STRATEGIC — Moscow escalates coercive warnings around threatened strikes on Kyiv

title: Russian Iskander strike destroys UN World Food Programme aid warehouse in Dnipro
detail: A Russian Iskander ballistic missile struck a clearly marked UN World Food Programme warehouse in Dnipro on May 25, destroying humanitarian food supplies intended for 130,000 people near the front line and valued at about $1.4 million. WFP said no staff were injured, but the attack fit a wider pattern of strikes on humanitarian warehouses, distribution points and vehicles; Ukrainian officials condemned it as a blatant violation of international humanitarian law.
tn: UN World Food Programme warehouse, Dnipro, Ukraine
dl: HEAVY — UN-marked humanitarian warehouse destroyed, eliminating food aid for 130,000 people

title: Russian attacks kill eight and injure 105 across Ukraine as 122-drone and Iskander strike follows Kyiv barrage
detail: Russian attacks killed eight civilians and injured 105 others across eight Ukrainian regions over the past day, according to regional authorities summarized by the Kyiv Independent. Ukraine's Air Force said Russia launched two Iskander-M ballistic missiles and 122 drones, including Shahed-type UAVs, overnight on May 26; air defenses intercepted or suppressed 111 drones, while nine struck targets across 11 locations and debris fell at three additional sites. Kramatorsk suffered the heaviest single attack, with at least seven aerial bombs killing two people and wounding 18, while Kharkiv Oblast reported three killed and 33 injured, including a missile strike on a civilian business in Derhachi that killed two and wounded 24; Odesa, Dnipropetrovsk, Kherson, Sumy and Zaporizhzhia oblasts also reported casualties and infrastructure damage.
tn: Kramatorsk, Donetsk Oblast, Ukraine and multiple regions
dl: HEAVY — Nationwide Russian missile, bomb and drone attacks cause mass civilian casualties

title: General Staff reports 267 frontline clashes, 47 Russian assaults repelled near Pokrovsk and 24 near Huliaipole
detail: Ukraine's General Staff reported 267 combat engagements over the previous day, with the most intense Russian pressure again concentrated on the Pokrovsk and Huliaipole sectors. Russian forces launched four missiles, 79 airstrikes with 254 guided aerial bombs, 8,684 kamikaze drones and 2,848 shelling attacks, including 54 MLRS attacks. Ukrainian defenders reported stopping 47 assault attempts on the Pokrovsk axis near settlements including Shakhove, Vilne, Rodynske, Novooleksandrivka, Hryshyne, Udachne and Novopavlivka, and 24 assaults on the Huliaipole axis near Dobropillia, Nove Zaporizhzhia, Zaliznychne, Rivnopillia and other settlements.
tn: Pokrovsk and Huliaipole axes, eastern and southern Ukraine
dl: SIGNIFICANT — High-intensity assaults continue with main pressure on Pokrovsk and Huliaipole axes

title: Russian forces hit 34 Zaporizhzhia settlements 616 times, wounding 15 civilians in the regional center
detail: Zaporizhzhia regional authorities said Russian forces carried out 616 strikes on 34 settlements over the past day, including one missile strike on Zaporizhzhia city, 18 airstrikes, 392 drone attacks and 205 artillery strikes. The missile strike on the regional center wounded 15 civilians, including an infant, while officials logged 89 reports of damage to infrastructure, homes, vehicles and utility buildings across the oblast.
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Hundreds of missile, air, drone and artillery strikes wound civilians and damage regional infrastructure

title: Russia launches 163-drone overnight attack across Ukraine; 150 intercepted or jammed
detail: Russia launched 163 Shahed-type, Gerbera, Italmas and Parodiya decoy drones from Kursk, Bryansk, Oryol, Millerovo and Shatalovo beginning on the evening of May 26. Ukraine's Air Force said air defenses, electronic warfare units, unmanned systems and mobile fire groups destroyed or jammed 150 drones by 08:30 on May 27, while eight strike UAVs hit seven locations and debris fell at four more; several drones remained in Ukrainian airspace during the morning update.
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Large drone barrage mostly intercepted but hits recorded at seven locations

title: Russian drone wave hits Chernihiv industrial and residential areas, damaging warehouses, offices and homes
detail: Chernihiv officials reported around 15 explosions during Russia's overnight attack on May 26-27. The strike damaged an industrial business, warehouse premises, an office building, machinery and vehicles, as well as five dacha houses and six multi-storey residential buildings where windows were blown out. Local authorities reported no casualties, but the attack underscored continued targeting of northern Ukrainian cities during the wider 163-drone barrage.
dl: MODERATE — Industrial and residential damage after roughly 15 explosions, with no casualties reported

title: Ukraine strikes Russian aircraft repair plant, Voronezh airbase area, Tuapse refinery and occupied Crimea targets
detail: Ukrainian long-range strikes hit or targeted multiple Russian military and energy sites early on May 27, including the area of the 325th Aviation Repair Plant in Taganrog, the Baltimor military airfield area in Voronezh, the Tuapse oil refinery on the Black Sea coast and targets in occupied Sevastopol. Russian officials claimed extensive interceptions, including 140 drones over Russian regions and occupied Crimea, while local footage and monitoring channels reported fires, explosions and damage near strategic facilities used to support Russian air operations and logistics.
tn: Taganrog, Voronezh, Tuapse and occupied Sevastopol
dl: HEAVY — Coordinated Ukrainian deep-strike wave targets Russian aircraft repair, airbase, oil and occupation infrastructure

title: General Staff reports 296 frontline clashes, with 53 Russian assaults repelled near Pokrovsk and 35 near Huliaipole
detail: Ukraine's General Staff reported 296 combat engagements over the previous day, with Russia conducting 76 airstrikes that dropped 239 guided bombs, 7,089 kamikaze drone attacks and 2,568 shelling attacks, including 36 from MLRS. Ukrainian defenders said they repelled 53 Russian assault and offensive actions on the Pokrovsk axis and 35 on the Huliaipole axis, while the Kostiantynivka sector also saw 26 attacks and the North Slobozhanshchyna/Kursk area recorded nine clashes.
tn: Pokrovsk and Huliaipole axes, eastern and southern Ukraine
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Nearly 300 reported frontline clashes, with pressure again concentrated on Pokrovsk and Huliaipole axes

title: ISW says Ukraine's mid-range and frontline drone campaign is disrupting Russian logistics and fueling possible Russian export curbs
detail: The Institute for the Study of War assessed on May 27 that Ukraine's intensified mid-range and frontline drone strike campaigns are limiting Russia's ability to move personnel to the front and sustain positions, including by degrading ground lines of communication such as the M-14 Rostov-Crimea highway and routes linking occupied southern Ukraine with Donetsk City. ISW also reported that Moscow was considering temporary restrictions on diesel and jet-fuel exports after months of Ukrainian strikes on Russian refineries, indicating Kyiv's strike campaign is imposing costs on Russia's fuel industry and forcing tradeoffs among domestic stability, military logistics and export revenue.
tn: Southern occupied Ukraine logistics routes and Russian fuel sector
dl: STRATEGIC — Ukrainian drone and refinery-strike campaigns increasingly pressure Russian logistics and fuel policy

sources.name: Institute for the Study of War

title: Russian MLRS strike on Kherson playground kills father and wounds mother and two young children
detail: Russian forces struck a residential neighborhood in Kherson's Korabelnyi district with multiple-launch rocket systems on the evening of May 27, hitting a children's playground, according to local authorities cited by the Kyiv Independent. The strike killed a father at the scene and seriously wounded his wife and their two daughters, aged three and six; the three-year-old was reported in critical condition with blast and shrapnel wounds. The attack came amid wider Russian strikes across Ukraine that killed at least two people and injured 23 over the past day, including 12 wounded across Kherson Oblast and additional casualties in Donetsk, Dnipropetrovsk, Zaporizhzhia, Sumy and Kharkiv oblasts.
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Strike on playground kills one civilian and seriously wounds a family including two small children

title: Russia attacks Ukraine with Kinzhal missile and 147 drones; Ukraine says 138 drones destroyed or jammed
detail: Russia launched a Kinzhal air-launched ballistic missile from Lipetsk Oblast and 147 drones overnight on May 27-28, including Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas and other types from Bryansk, Kursk, Oryol, Millerovo, Primorsko-Akhtarsk and occupied Crimea. Ukraine's Air Force said aircraft, air-defense missile units, electronic warfare teams, unmanned systems units and mobile fire groups destroyed or jammed 138 drones by 08:00. Hits by the Kinzhal and nine UAVs were recorded at seven locations, with debris from downed aerial targets falling at six more sites.
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Mixed ballistic-missile and drone strike records hits at seven locations despite widespread interceptions

title: Ukrainian drones hit Russian Nebo-SV radar, Buk-M2 command vehicle, S-350 support vehicle and logistics sites in occupied regions
detail: Ukraine's Unmanned Systems Forces reported a series of coordinated strikes in temporarily occupied Luhansk, Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia regions against Russian air-defense and logistics targets. Reported hits included a Nebo-SV radar station in Luhansk Oblast, a Buk-M2 air-defense command-and-staff vehicle, an S-350 Vityaz tractor/support vehicle in Donetsk Oblast, logistics and supply warehouses, temporary deployment points and other logistics facilities. The operation involved the 1st Separate Center, the 414th 'Birds of Magyar' Brigade's 9th Kairos Battalion, the 413th 'Raid' Regiment and the 412th Nemesis Brigade in coordination with the Deep Strike Center.
tn: Occupied Luhansk, Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia regions
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Drone strikes target Russian air-defense command, radar and logistics infrastructure across occupied regions

title: General Staff reports 317 frontline clashes, with 66 Russian assault actions stopped in Pokrovsk sector
detail: Ukraine's General Staff reported 317 combat engagements over the previous day, with the Pokrovsk sector remaining the hottest area. Russian forces launched 68 airstrikes using 202 guided aerial bombs, deployed 8,359 kamikaze drones and carried out 2,714 shelling attacks, including 45 using MLRS. Ukrainian defenders said they stopped 66 Russian assault actions in the Pokrovsk sector, 35 attacks in the Huliaipole sector, 15 in the Kostiantynivka sector and additional attacks across the southern Slobozhanshchyna, Kupiansk, Lyman, Sloviansk, Kramatorsk and Orikhiv sectors.
tn: Pokrovsk and Huliaipole axes, eastern and southern Ukraine
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Frontline clash count rises above 300, with Pokrovsk again absorbing the heaviest Russian pressure

title: Zelenskyy asks Trump and Congress for Patriot interceptors, calling Russian ballistic missiles Putin's last major battlefield advantage
detail: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy sent a letter to U.S. President Donald Trump and Congress seeking urgent help with air-defense systems and interceptor missiles, saying Russia's ballistic missiles remain Vladimir Putin's 'last major advantage on the battlefield.' Zelenskyy said Ukraine was ready to purchase the Patriot systems and interceptors it needs but warned that the NATO PURL delivery pace was not keeping up with the threat. The appeal followed a massive May 24 strike in which Russia used 30 ballistic missiles, only 11 of which were intercepted, and Zelenskyy argued that stronger ballistic-missile defense was essential to forcing Moscow into genuine diplomacy.
dl: STRATEGIC — Ukraine links urgent Patriot interceptor supplies to restoring leverage for peace talks

title: Sweden pledges 16 donated Gripen C/D jets and Ukraine purchase of 20 Gripen E fighters
detail: Sweden and Ukraine announced a Gripen fighter package: Sweden will donate 16 older Gripen C/D aircraft expected in 2027 while Ukraine will buy 20 new Gripen E jets for delivery from 2030, a long-term boost to Ukraine's air-defense and fighter fleet plans.
dl: LIGHT — Major future air-capability pledge

title: Ukraine strikes Russian ST-68 radar in occupied Crimea and drone-control, logistics and ammunition sites in Donetsk region
detail: Ukraine's General Staff said Ukrainian forces hit an ST-68 air target detection radar at occupied Feodosia, drone control centers near Kalynove and Novohrodivka, supply depots near Mariupol, Novoselivka Druha and Buhas, and an ammunition depot near Hrabove; a separate May 27 strike reportedly destroyed a Russian command post near Paraskoviivka with 18 irrecoverable losses.
tn: Feodosia, occupied Crimea and occupied Donetsk region
dl: HEAVY — Air-defense, command and logistics targets hit

title: Russian FPV drone kills two civilians on field road in Sumy region's Velyka Pysarivka community
detail: Sumy prosecutors said Russian forces used a drone to attack two civilians traveling along a field road in the Velyka Pysarivka community of Okhtyrka district, killing a man and a woman, amid more than 60 reported Russian attacks on Sumy settlements over the previous day.
tn: Velyka Pysarivka community, Sumy Oblast, Ukraine
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Civilian vehicle targeted by drone

title: Russia launches Iskander/S-400 ballistic missile and 232 drones; Ukraine says 217 UAVs neutralized
detail: Ukraine's Air Force said Russia fired one Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missile from Kursk region and 232 Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas and decoy drones from Russian launch areas and occupied Crimea. Ukrainian defenses shot down or jammed 217 drones, while the missile and 14 strike drones hit 14 locations and debris fell in seven others as the attack continued into the morning.
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Large drone-missile wave with hits at 14 sites

title: General Staff reports 267 frontline clashes, with heaviest fighting in Pokrovsk and Huliaipole sectors
detail: Ukraine's General Staff reported 267 combat clashes over the previous day, including 46 Russian assault attempts stopped on the Pokrovsk axis and 32 assaults on the Huliaipole axis. Russian forces also carried out one missile strike, 86 airstrikes with 269 guided bombs, 7,881 kamikaze-drone uses and 2,806 shelling attacks.
tn: Pokrovsk and Huliaipole axes, eastern and southern Ukraine
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Sustained high-tempo assaults across front
